# dukdb

```go
import "github.com/dukdb/dukdb-go"
```

Package dukdb provides a database/sql driver for DuckDB.

This package is a pure Go implementation that maintains API compatibility with the original duckdb\-go driver while requiring zero cgo dependencies.

The driver is registered with database/sql under the name "dukdb".

### Usage

To use this driver, import it in your application:

```
import (
    "database/sql"
    _ "github.com/dukdb/dukdb-go"
)
```

Then open a database connection:

```
// In-memory database
db, err := sql.Open("dukdb", ":memory:")

// File-based database
db, err := sql.Open("dukdb", "/path/to/database.db")
```

### Backend Interface

The driver uses a pluggable backend architecture. By default, a backend must be registered that provides the actual DuckDB implementation. The Backend interface defines how the driver communicates with DuckDB.

### Configuration

Database configuration options can be passed via the DSN query string:

```
db, err := sql.Open("dukdb", ":memory:?access_mode=read_write&threads=4")
```

Supported configuration options include:

- access\_mode: "read\_only" or "read\_write" \(default: "read\_write"\)
- threads: Worker thread count \(0 = auto, 1\-128 = explicit\)
- max\_memory: Memory limit \("1GB", "512MB", "80%"\)
- default\_order: "asc" or "desc" \(default: "asc"\)

Package dukdb provides a pure Go implementation of a DuckDB\-compatible database driver.

<details><summary>Example (Nested Types)</summary>
<p>

Example\_nestedTypes demonstrates creating nested type structures.

```go
// Create a complex nested type: MAP[VARCHAR, LIST[STRUCT(id INTEGER, name VARCHAR)]]

// Build from innermost to outermost:
intInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_INTEGER,
)
strInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_VARCHAR,
)

// 1. Create the inner struct
idEntry, _ := dukdb.NewStructEntry(
	intInfo,
	"id",
)
nameEntry, _ := dukdb.NewStructEntry(
	strInfo,
	"name",
)
structInfo, _ := dukdb.NewStructInfo(
	idEntry,
	nameEntry,
)

// 2. Wrap in a list
listInfo, _ := dukdb.NewListInfo(structInfo)

// 3. Create the map
mapInfo, _ := dukdb.NewMapInfo(
	strInfo,
	listInfo,
)

fmt.Println("SQL:", mapInfo.SQLType())

// Output:
// SQL: MAP(VARCHAR, STRUCT("id" INTEGER, "name" VARCHAR)[])
```

#### Output

```
SQL: MAP(VARCHAR, STRUCT("id" INTEGER, "name" VARCHAR)[])
```

</p>
</details>

## Constants

<a name="minThresholdBytes"></a>Constants for threshold validation

```go
const (
    minThresholdBytes   = 1024    // 1 KB minimum
    minRecommendedBytes = 1048576 // 1 MB recommended minimum
)
```

<a name="driverErrMsg"></a>Error message constants for TypeInfo.

```go
const (
    driverErrMsg          = "database/sql/driver"
    unsupportedTypeErrMsg = "unsupported data type"
    tryOtherFuncErrMsg    = "please try this function instead"
    indexErrMsg           = "index"
    interfaceIsNilErrMsg  = "interface is nil"
    duplicateNameErrMsg   = "duplicate name"
)
```

<a name="DefaultAppenderThreshold"></a>DefaultAppenderThreshold is the default number of rows buffered before auto\-flush.

```go
const DefaultAppenderThreshold = 1024
```

<a name="DefaultFileGlobTimeout"></a>DefaultFileGlobTimeout is the default timeout in seconds for cloud storage glob operations.

```go
const DefaultFileGlobTimeout = 60
```

<a name="DefaultMaxFilesPerGlob"></a>DefaultMaxFilesPerGlob is the default limit on files matched by a glob pattern. This limit prevents memory exhaustion when glob patterns match too many files.

```go
const DefaultMaxFilesPerGlob = 10000
```

<a name="UhugeintMaxString"></a>UhugeintMaxString is the maximum value for UHUGEINT as a string. Max value: 2^128 \- 1 = 340282366920938463463374607431768211455

```go
const UhugeintMaxString = "340282366920938463463374607431768211455"
```

<a name="VectorSize"></a>VectorSize is the default number of values in a vector \(matching DuckDB's VECTOR\_SIZE\).

```go
const VectorSize = 2048
```

<a name="bitsPerWord"></a>bitsPerWord is the number of bits in a uint64 \(used for validity bitmaps\).

```go
const bitsPerWord = 64
```

<a name="maxDecimalWidth"></a>maxDecimalWidth is the maximum width for DECIMAL types.

```go
const maxDecimalWidth = 38
```

<a name="maxFileGlobTimeout"></a>maxFileGlobTimeout is the maximum allowed timeout in seconds \(10 minutes\).

```go
const maxFileGlobTimeout = 600
```

<a name="maxMaxFilesPerGlob"></a>maxMaxFilesPerGlob is the maximum allowed value for max\_files\_per\_glob.

```go
const maxMaxFilesPerGlob = 1000000
```

<a name="maxThreads"></a>maxThreads is the maximum number of threads allowed.

```go
const maxThreads = 128
```

<a name="minFileGlobTimeout"></a>minFileGlobTimeout is the minimum allowed timeout in seconds.

```go
const minFileGlobTimeout = 1
```

<a name="minMaxFilesPerGlob"></a>minMaxFilesPerGlob is the minimum allowed value for max\_files\_per\_glob.

```go
const minMaxFilesPerGlob = 1
```

<a name="minThreads"></a>minThreads is the minimum number of threads allowed.

```go
const minThreads = 1
```

<a name="nanosPerHour"></a>nanosPerHour is used for TimeNS calculations.

```go
const nanosPerHour = 60 * nanosPerMinute
```

<a name="nanosPerMinute"></a>nanosPerMinute is used for TimeNS calculations.

```go
const nanosPerMinute = 60 * nanosPerSecond
```

<a name="nanosPerSecond"></a>nanosPerSecond is used for TimeNS calculations.

```go
const nanosPerSecond = 1_000_000_000
```

<a name="secondsPerDay"></a>secondsPerDay is used for date calculations.

```go
const secondsPerDay = 86400
```

<a name="uuidLength"></a>

```go
const uuidLength = 16
```

## Variables

<a name="_"></a>Compile\-time assertions

```go
var (
    _   array.RecordReader = (*arrowRecordReader)(
        nil,
    )
    _   VirtualTable = (*arrowVirtualTable)(
        nil,
    )
    _   RowIterator = (*arrowRowIterator)(
        nil,
    )
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Interface assertions to verify that binding types implement driver.Valuer.

```go
var (
    _   driver.Valuer = ListValue[int](nil)
    _   driver.Valuer = StructValue[struct{}]{}
    _   driver.Valuer = MapValue[string, int](nil)
    _   driver.Valuer = ArrayValue[int](nil)
    _   driver.Valuer = JSONValue[struct{}]{}
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Ensure interfaces are implemented.

```go
var (
    _   driver.Conn               = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.ConnPrepareContext = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.ExecerContext      = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.QueryerContext     = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.ConnBeginTx        = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.NamedValueChecker  = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.Pinger             = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.SessionResetter    = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.Validator          = (*Conn)(nil)
    _   driver.Tx                 = (*tx)(nil)
    _   driver.Result             = (*result)(nil)
    _   driver.Rows               = (*rows)(nil)
    _   driver.Stmt               = (*Stmt)(nil)
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Ensure Driver implements the required interfaces.

```go
var (
    _   driver.Driver        = (*Driver)(nil)
    _   driver.DriverContext = (*Driver)(nil)
)
```

<a name="errConnect"></a>Common error variables for internal use.

```go
var (
    errConnect = errors.New(
        "could not connect to database",
    )
    errParseDSN = errors.New(
        "could not parse DSN for database",
    )
    errClosedCon = errors.New(
        "closed connection",
    )
    errNoBackend = errors.New(
        "no backend registered",
    )
    errClosedStmt = errors.New("closed statement")
    errBeginTx    = errors.New(
        "could not begin transaction",
    )
    errMultipleTx = errors.New(
        "multiple transactions",
    )
)
```

<a name="ErrConnectionClosed"></a>Exported error variables for use by backend implementations.

```go
var (
    // ErrConnectionClosed indicates the connection has been closed.
    ErrConnectionClosed = errors.New(
        "connection closed",
    )

    // ErrTransactionAlreadyEnded indicates the transaction has already been committed or rolled back.
    ErrTransactionAlreadyEnded = errors.New(
        "transaction already ended",
    )

    // ErrTableNotFound indicates the specified table was not found.
    ErrTableNotFound = errors.New(
        "table not found",
    )

    // ErrTableAlreadyExists indicates the table already exists.
    ErrTableAlreadyExists = errors.New(
        "table already exists",
    )

    // ErrColumnNotFound indicates the specified column was not found.
    ErrColumnNotFound = errors.New(
        "column not found",
    )

    // ErrTypeMismatch indicates a type mismatch error.
    ErrTypeMismatch = errors.New("type mismatch")

    // ErrInvalidSyntax indicates a SQL syntax error.
    ErrInvalidSyntax = errors.New(
        "invalid syntax",
    )

    // ErrNotImplemented indicates the feature is not implemented.
    ErrNotImplemented = errors.New(
        "not implemented",
    )

    // ErrDivisionByZero indicates a division by zero error.
    ErrDivisionByZero = errors.New(
        "division by zero",
    )

    // ErrNullConstraint indicates a NULL constraint violation.
    ErrNullConstraint = errors.New(
        "NULL constraint violation",
    )

    // ErrNotSupported indicates the feature is not supported by the backend.
    ErrNotSupported = errors.New(
        "not supported",
    )
)
```

<a name="errAPI"></a>TypeInfo\-specific error variables \(for API compatibility with duckdb\-go\).

```go
var (
    // errAPI is the base error for API errors.
    errAPI = errors.New("API error")

    // errEmptyName indicates an empty name was provided.
    errEmptyName = errors.New("empty name")

    // errInvalidDecimalWidth indicates an invalid decimal width.
    errInvalidDecimalWidth = fmt.Errorf(
        "the DECIMAL width must be between 1 and %d",
        maxDecimalWidth,
    )

    // errInvalidDecimalScale indicates an invalid decimal scale.
    errInvalidDecimalScale = errors.New(
        "the DECIMAL scale must be less than or equal to the width",
    )

    // errInvalidArraySize indicates an invalid array size.
    errInvalidArraySize = errors.New(
        "invalid ARRAY size",
    )
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Ensure PreparedStmt implements the driver.Stmt interface.

```go
var (
    _   driver.Stmt = (*PreparedStmt)(
        nil,
    )
    _   driver.StmtExecContext = (*PreparedStmt)(
        nil,
    )
    _   driver.StmtQueryContext = (*PreparedStmt)(
        nil,
    )
)
```

<a name="errProfilingNotEnabled"></a>Error variables for profiling operations.

```go
var (
    errProfilingNotEnabled = errors.New(
        "profiling is not enabled",
    )
    errProfilingInfoEmpty = errors.New(
        "profiling info is empty",
    )
)
```

<a name="errReplacementScanUnsupportedType"></a>Error variables for replacement scan operations.

```go
var (
    errReplacementScanUnsupportedType = errors.New(
        "replacement scan: unsupported parameter type",
    )
    errReplacementScanFailed = errors.New(
        "replacement scan callback failed",
    )
)
```

```go
var (

    reflectTypeBool = reflect.TypeOf(

    reflectTypeUint8 = reflect.TypeOf(

    )
    reflectTypeUint16 = reflect.TypeOf(

    )
    reflectTypeUint32 = reflect.TypeOf(
        uint32(0),
    )
    reflectTypeUint64 = reflect.TypeOf(
        uint64(0),
    )
    reflectTypeFloat32 = reflect.TypeOf(
        float32(0),
    )
    reflectTypeFloat64 = reflect.TypeOf(
        float64(0),
    )
    reflectTypeString = reflect.TypeOf("")
    reflectTypeBytes  = reflect.TypeOf(
        []byte{},
    )
    reflectTypeTime = reflect.TypeOf(
        time.Time{},
    )
    reflectTypeUUID = reflect.TypeOf(
        UUID{},
    )
    reflectTypeInterval = reflect.TypeOf(

    )
    reflectTypeDecimal = reflect.TypeOf(
        Decimal{},
    )
    reflectTypeBigInt = reflect.TypeOf(

    )
    reflectTypeMapStringAny = reflect.TypeOf(

    )
    reflectTypeMap = reflect.TypeOf(

    )
    reflectTypeUnion = reflect.TypeOf(
```

<a name="_"></a>Interface assertions to verify Rows implements the expected interfaces.

```go
var (
    _   driver.Rows = (*Rows)(
        nil,
    )
    _   driver.RowsColumnTypeScanType = (*Rows)(
        nil,
    )
    _   driver.RowsColumnTypeDatabaseTypeName = (*Rows)(
        nil,
    )
    _   driver.RowsColumnTypeNullable = (*Rows)(
        nil,
    )
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Interface assertions to verify that scanner types implement sql.Scanner.

```go
var (
    _   sql.Scanner = (*ListScanner[int])(nil)
    _   sql.Scanner = (*ArrayScanner[int])(nil)
    _   sql.Scanner = (*StructScanner[struct{}])(
        nil,
    )
    _   sql.Scanner = (*MapScanner[string, int])(
        nil,
    )
    _   sql.Scanner = (*UnionScanner)(nil)
    _   sql.Scanner = (*EnumScanner[string])(nil)
    _   sql.Scanner = (*JSONScanner[struct{}])(nil)
    _   sql.Scanner = (*UUIDScanner)(nil)
    _   sql.Scanner = (*TimeNSScanner)(nil)
)
```

<a name="errTableUDFNoName"></a>Error variables for table UDF operations.

```go
var (
    errTableUDFNoName = errors.New(
        "table UDF name cannot be empty",
    )
    errTableUDFMissingBindArgs = errors.New(
        "table UDF requires BindArguments or BindArgumentsContext",
    )
    errTableUDFArgumentIsNil = errors.New(
        "table UDF argument type cannot be nil",
    )
    errTableUDFColumnTypeIsNil = errors.New(
        "table UDF column type cannot be nil",
    )
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Interface assertions.

```go
var (
    _   sql.Scanner   = (*Uhugeint)(nil)
    _   driver.Valuer = Uhugeint{}
    _   sql.Scanner   = (*Bit)(nil)
    _   driver.Valuer = Bit{}
    _   sql.Scanner   = (*TimeNS)(nil)
    _   driver.Valuer = TimeNS(0)
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Interface assertions to verify that types implement the expected interfaces. These are compile\-time checks \- if a type does not implement an interface, the code will not compile.

```go
var (
    // UUID implements both sql.Scanner and driver.Valuer
    _   sql.Scanner   = (*UUID)(nil)
    _   driver.Valuer = (*UUID)(nil)

    // Map implements sql.Scanner only (read-only from query results)
    _   sql.Scanner = (*Map)(nil)
)
```

<a name="_"></a>Ensure Connector implements driver.Connector.

```go
var _ driver.Connector = (*Connector)(nil)
```

```go
var _ = ptrAdd
```

<a name="BindParams"></a>
## func [BindParams](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/params.go#L155-L158>)

```go
func BindParams(query string, args []driver.NamedValue) (string, error)
```

BindParams binds the given arguments to the query placeholders and returns the query with all placeholders replaced by literal values. It supports both positional \($1, $2, etc. \- 1\-based\) and named \(@name\) placeholders, but mixing the two styles is not allowed.

<a name="ClearTypeInfoCache"></a>
## func [ClearTypeInfoCache](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info_cache.go#L71>)

```go
func ClearTypeInfoCache()
```

ClearTypeInfoCache clears the primitive type info cache. This is primarily useful for testing.

<a name="ConnId"></a>
## func [ConnId](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connid.go#L82>)

```go
func ConnId(c *sql.Conn) (uint64, error)
```

ConnId returns the unique connection ID for a database connection.

Each connection is assigned a unique, monotonically increasing ID when created. This ID remains stable throughout the connection's lifetime and is never reused within a single process.

### Use Cases

The ID is useful for:

- Debugging and tracing queries to specific connections
- Connection pool management and monitoring
- Correlating connection\-specific operations
- Query tracing with connection attribution
- Multi\-tenant connection isolation

### Thread Safety

ConnId is safe to call concurrently from multiple goroutines. The underlying ID is a uint64 stored in the backend connection and is assigned atomically during connection creation.

### ID Space and Wraparound

Connection IDs are 64\-bit unsigned integers starting at 1. With a uint64, wraparound would only occur after 18 quintillion \(2^64\) connections, which is not a practical concern for any real\-world application. IDs increment monotonically and are never reused within a process lifetime.

### Errors

Returns an error if:

- The connection is nil \("connection is nil"\)
- The connection has been closed \("closed connection"\)
- The connection is not a dukdb connection \("not a DukDB driver connection"\)
- The backend does not support identification \("backend does not support connection identification"\)

On error, the returned ID is always 0 \(an invalid ID value, since IDs start at 1\).

### Example

```
db, _ := sql.Open("dukdb", ":memory:")
defer db.Close()

conn, _ := db.Conn(context.Background())
defer conn.Close()

id, err := dukdb.ConnId(conn)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatalf("failed to get connection ID: %v", err)
}
fmt.Printf("Connection ID: %d\n", id)
```

### Example: Connection Pool Tracking

```
// Track active connections in a pool
activeConns := make(map[uint64]*sql.Conn)
var mu sync.Mutex

conn, _ := db.Conn(ctx)
id, _ := dukdb.ConnId(conn)

mu.Lock()
activeConns[id] = conn
mu.Unlock()
```

### Example: Query Tracing

```
func execWithTrace(conn *sql.Conn, query string) error {
    id, _ := dukdb.ConnId(conn)
    log.Printf("[conn:%d] executing: %s", id, query)
    _, err := conn.ExecContext(context.Background(), query)
    log.Printf("[conn:%d] completed (err=%v)", id, err)
    return err
}
```


<a name="DataChunkToRecordBatch"></a>
## func [DataChunkToRecordBatch](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/arrow_convert.go#L18-L22>)

```go
func DataChunkToRecordBatch(chunk *DataChunk, schema *arrow.Schema, alloc memory.Allocator) (arrow.RecordBatch, error)
```

DataChunkToRecordBatch converts a DataChunk to an Arrow RecordBatch. Uses copy semantics for memory safety \(Arrow and Go have different buffer management\). The caller is responsible for releasing the returned Record.

```go
func GetDataChunkCapacity() int
```

<a name="GetTableNames"></a>
## func [GetTableNames](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/gettablenames.go#L76>)

```go
func GetTableNames(conn *sql.Conn, query string, qualified bool) ([]string, error)
```

GetTableNames extracts table names referenced in a SQL query.

This function parses the given SQL query and returns a sorted, deduplicated list of table names that are referenced in the query. The conn parameter is used to access the underlying backend connection for query parsing.

### Supported Statement Types

The following statement types are fully supported:

- SELECT \(including JOINs, subqueries in WHERE/HAVING, scalar subqueries\)
- INSERT \(target table and SELECT subquery if present\)
- UPDATE \(target table and WHERE clause subqueries\)
- DELETE \(target table and WHERE clause subqueries\)
- CREATE TABLE \(the table being created\)
- DROP TABLE \(the table being dropped\)

### Not Supported

The following features are NOT currently supported:

- Common Table Expressions \(CTEs / WITH clauses\) \- requires AST enhancement
- UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT queries \- parsed as single SELECT
- CREATE TABLE AS SELECT \- only extracts target table, not SELECT source tables
- UPDATE ... FROM \- requires AST enhancement for FROM clause in UPDATE

### Edge Cases

- Empty query: returns \[\]string\{\}, nil \(not nil slice\)
- Query with no tables \(e.g., "SELECT 1"\): returns \[\]string\{\}, nil
- Table functions \(e.g., read\_parquet\(\)\): excluded \(not real table references\)
- Multiple statements: only the first statement is processed
- Parse errors: returns nil, error with "parse error:" prefix

### Qualified Names

When qualified is false, only the unqualified table name is returned \(e.g., "users"\). When qualified is true, the full qualified name is returned \(e.g., "main.public.users"\).

### Examples

Basic usage:

```
tables, err := dukdb.GetTableNames(conn, "SELECT * FROM users", false)
// tables = []string{"users"}
```

Multiple tables with JOINs:

```
tables, err := dukdb.GetTableNames(conn, "SELECT * FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id", false)
// tables = []string{"customers", "orders"} (sorted alphabetically)
```

Qualified names with schema:

```
tables, err := dukdb.GetTableNames(conn, "SELECT * FROM public.users", true)
// tables = []string{"public.users"}
```

Subquery extraction:

```
tables, err := dukdb.GetTableNames(conn, "SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT id FROM customers)", false)
// tables = []string{"customers", "orders"}
```

Parameters:

- conn: A \*sql.Conn instance. Used to access the underlying backend connection.
- query: The SQL query string to parse.
- qualified: If true, returns qualified table names \(schema.table\); if false, returns unqualified names.

Returns:

- A sorted, deduplicated slice of table names, or an empty slice if none are found.
- An error if the query cannot be parsed or if the backend doesn't support table extraction.

<a name="ParseThreshold"></a>
## func [ParseThreshold](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/config.go#L550>)

```go
func ParseThreshold(value string) (int64, error)
```

ParseThreshold parses a threshold value string and returns the size in bytes. Supports suffixes: b, kb, mb, gb \(case\-insensitive\) Plain numbers are treated as bytes. Examples:

- "1024b" → 1024
- "512KB" → 524288
- "256MB" → 268435456
- "1GB" → 1073741824
- "1000000" → 1000000

<a name="ParseValue"></a>
## func [ParseValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_json.go#L17-L20>)

```go
func ParseValue(data []byte, typ Type) (any, error)
```

ParseValue parses JSON data according to the specified DuckDB type. This function handles DuckDB CLI JSON output format, including special representations for floats \(Infinity, \-Infinity, NaN\), BLOBs \(\\x hex format\), and nested types \(LIST, STRUCT, MAP, UNION\).

<a name="RecordBatchToDataChunk"></a>
## func [RecordBatchToDataChunk](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/arrow_convert.go#L423-L426>)

```go
func RecordBatchToDataChunk(record arrow.RecordBatch, chunk *DataChunk) error
```

RecordBatchToDataChunk converts an Arrow RecordBatch to a DataChunk. The chunk must be pre\-initialized with the correct column types. Uses copy semantics for memory safety.


```go
func RegisterBackend(b Backend)
```

RegisterBackend registers a backend implementation. This should be called by backend implementations in their init\(\) function.

<a name="RegisterReplacementScan"></a>
## func [RegisterReplacementScan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L111-L114>)

```go
func RegisterReplacementScan(c *Connector, callback ReplacementScanCallback)
```

RegisterReplacementScan registers a replacement scan callback for the connector. The callback is called during query binding when a table reference is encountered. Only one callback can be registered per connector; subsequent calls replace the previous callback.

<a name="RegisterScalarUDF"></a>
## func [RegisterScalarUDF](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L484-L488>)

```go
func RegisterScalarUDF(c *sql.Conn, name string, f ScalarFunc) error
```

RegisterScalarUDF registers a user\-defined scalar function. c is the SQL connection on which to register the scalar function. name is the function name, and f is the scalar function's interface ScalarFunc.

<a name="RegisterScalarUDFSet"></a>
## func [RegisterScalarUDFSet](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L511-L515>)

```go
func RegisterScalarUDFSet(c *sql.Conn, name string, functions ...ScalarFunc) error
```

RegisterScalarUDFSet registers a set of user\-defined scalar functions with the same name. This enables overloading of scalar functions. c is the SQL connection on which to register the scalar function set. name is the function name of each function in the set. functions contains all ScalarFunc functions of the scalar function set.

<a name="RegisterTableUDF"></a>

```go
func RegisterTableUDF[TFT TableFunction](conn *sql.Conn, name string, f TFT) error
```

RegisterTableUDF registers a user\-defined table function. Projection pushdown is enabled by default.

<a name="ResolveMaxMemory"></a>

```go
func ResolveMaxMemory(value string) (int64, error)
```

ResolveMaxMemory resolves a max\_memory value to bytes. If the value is a percentage, it returns the percentage of total system memory. Note: For percentage values, this function requires system memory information which may not be available on all platforms. In such cases, it returns an error.

<a name="SetChunkValue"></a>
## func [SetChunkValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L141-L145>)

```go
func SetChunkValue[T any](chunk DataChunk, colIdx, rowIdx int, val T) error
```

SetChunkValue writes a single value to a column in a data chunk. The difference with \`chunk.SetValue\` is that \`SetChunkValue\` does not require casting the value to \`any\` \(implicitly\). If the column is not projected, the value is ignored. NOTE: Custom ENUM types must be passed as string.

```go
func SetRowValue[T any](row Row, colIdx int, val T) error
```

SetRowValue is a generic function for type\-safe row value setting. If the column is not projected, the value is silently ignored.

<a name="ValidateThreshold"></a>
## func [ValidateThreshold](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/config.go#L602>)

```go
func ValidateThreshold(value string) error
```

ValidateThreshold validates a threshold value string. Returns an error if:

- The threshold is below 1KB \(absolute minimum\)

Warns if:

- The threshold is below 1MB \(recommended minimum\)

<a name="addIndexToError"></a>
## func [addIndexToError](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/errors.go#L302>)

```go
func addIndexToError(err error, idx int) error
```

<a name="countPlaceholders"></a>
## func [countPlaceholders](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L35>)

```go
func countPlaceholders(query string) int
```

countPlaceholders counts the number of placeholder parameters in a query. It returns the expected number of arguments for NumInput\(\): \- For positional placeholders \($1, $2, etc.\): returns the maximum index found \- For named placeholders \(@name\): returns the count of unique names \- For mixed placeholders: returns the sum \(execution will fail later\) \- Placeholders inside string literals are ignored

<a name="AggregateExecutionState"></a>
## type [AggregateExecutionState](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L370-L376>)

AggregateExecutionState manages all groups for aggregate execution. Clock is injected from Conn \-\> Engine \-\> ExecutionState for deterministic testing.

```go
type AggregateExecutionState struct {
    udf        *registeredAggregateFunc
    groups     map[string]*GroupState // Serialized key -> state
    noGroupKey *GroupState            // For ungrouped aggregations
    clock      quartz.Clock           // Injected from Conn.clock via query execution
    ctx        context.Context
}
```

<a name="NewAggregateExecutionState"></a>
### func [NewAggregateExecutionState](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L380-L384>)

```go
func NewAggregateExecutionState(udf *registeredAggregateFunc, ctx context.Context, clock quartz.Clock) *AggregateExecutionState
```

NewAggregateExecutionState creates execution state with injected clock. Clock flow: Conn.clock \-\> Engine.execute\(\) \-\> NewAggregateExecutionState\(clock\)

<a name="AggregateExecutionState.Cleanup"></a>
### func \(\*AggregateExecutionState\) [Cleanup](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L562>)

```go
func (s *AggregateExecutionState) Cleanup()
```

Cleanup destroys all group states \(called after results are collected\).

<a name="AggregateExecutionState.CombineWith"></a>
### func \(\*AggregateExecutionState\) [CombineWith](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L466-L468>)

```go
func (s *AggregateExecutionState) CombineWith(other *AggregateExecutionState) error
```

CombineWith merges another execution state \(for parallel aggregation\).

<a name="AggregateExecutionState.Finalize"></a>
### func \(\*AggregateExecutionState\) [Finalize](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L499>)

```go
func (s *AggregateExecutionState) Finalize() ([]GroupResult, error)
```

Finalize produces final results for all groups.

```go
func (s *AggregateExecutionState) ProcessChunk(chunk *DataChunk, groupCols []int, valueCols []int) error
```

<a name="AggregateFunc"></a>
## type [AggregateFunc](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L94-L97>)

AggregateFunc combines config and executor for a complete aggregate function definition.

```go
type AggregateFunc struct {
    Config   AggregateFuncConfig
    Executor AggregateFuncExecutor
}
```

<a name="AggregateFuncConfig"></a>
## type [AggregateFuncConfig](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L16-L38>)

AggregateFuncConfig defines aggregate function signature and behavior.

```go
type AggregateFuncConfig struct {
    // InputTypeInfos contains Type information for each input parameter of the aggregate function.
    // Cannot be empty, and each element cannot be nil.
    InputTypeInfos []TypeInfo

    // ResultTypeInfo holds the Type information of the aggregate function's result type.
    // Required, cannot be nil or TYPE_ANY.
    ResultTypeInfo TypeInfo

    // VariadicTypeInfo configures the number of input parameters.
    // If this field is nil, then the input parameters match InputTypeInfos.
    // Otherwise, the aggregate function's input parameters are set to variadic.
    VariadicTypeInfo TypeInfo

    // Volatile sets the stability of the aggregate function to volatile, if true.
    // Volatile aggregate functions skip result caching.
    Volatile bool

    // SpecialNullHandling disables the default NULL handling of aggregate functions, if true.
    // If false (default), NULL values are skipped in Update.
    // If true, NULL values are passed to Update.
    SpecialNullHandling bool
}
```

<a name="AggregateFuncExecutor"></a>
## type [AggregateFuncExecutor](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L66-L91>)

AggregateFuncExecutor contains the callbacks to execute a user\-defined aggregate function.

```go
type AggregateFuncExecutor struct {
    // Init creates initial state (REQUIRED).
    Init StateInitFn

    // Destroy cleans up state (optional, called during Cleanup).
    Destroy StateDestroyFn

    // Update processes input value (simple variant).
    // One of Update or UpdateCtx is REQUIRED.
    Update UpdateFn

    // UpdateCtx processes input value with context (context-aware variant).
    // One of Update or UpdateCtx is REQUIRED.
    UpdateCtx UpdateContextFn

    // Combine merges partial states (REQUIRED for parallel execution).
    Combine CombineFn

    // Finalize produces final result (simple variant).
    // One of Finalize or FinalizeCtx is REQUIRED.
    Finalize FinalizeFn

    // FinalizeCtx produces final result with context (context-aware variant).
    // One of Finalize or FinalizeCtx is REQUIRED.
    FinalizeCtx FinalizeContextFn
}
```

<a name="AggregateFuncState"></a>
## type [AggregateFuncState](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L42>)

AggregateFuncState is the user\-defined state type for aggregate functions. Users implement their own state type that will be used during aggregation.

```go
type AggregateFuncState interface{}
```

<a name="Appender"></a>
## type [Appender](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L25-L51>)

Appender provides efficient bulk data loading via batched INSERTs. For table appenders, it uses DataChunk\-based columnar buffering for performance. For query appenders, it uses row\-based buffering with SQL generation. Appender is thread\-safe and can be used from multiple goroutines.

There are two modes of operation:

- Table appender: Created via NewAppender, uses DataChunk\-based buffering
- Query appender: Created via NewQueryAppender, uses SQL\-based buffering

```go
type Appender struct {
    conn      *Conn    // Database connection
    catalog   string   // Database catalog (defaults to "memory")
    schema    string   // Schema name (defaults to "main")
    table     string   // Table name
    columns   []string // Column names in order
    colTypes  []Type   // Column types (for reference, validation is deferred)
    threshold int      // Auto-flush threshold
    closed    bool     // Whether the appender has been closed
    mu        sync.Mutex

    // DataChunk-based buffering for table appenders
    currentChunk   *DataChunk // Current DataChunk for columnar buffering
    currentSize    int        // Number of rows in currentChunk
    columnTypeInfo []TypeInfo // Column TypeInfo for DataChunk creation

    // Row-based buffering for query appenders (and fallback)
    buffer [][]any // Buffered rows (used by query appenders)

    // Query appender fields (only set when created via NewQueryAppender)
    isQueryAppender bool       // True if this is a query appender
    query           string     // The SQL query to execute (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE INTO)
    tempTableName   string     // Name of the temporary table for batched data
    queryColTypes   []TypeInfo // Column types for the temp table
    queryColNames   []string   // Column names for the temp table
    tempTableExists bool       // Whether the temp table has been created
}
```

<a name="NewAppender"></a>
### func [NewAppender](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L68-L71>)

```go
func NewAppender(conn *Conn, catalog, schema, table string) (*Appender, error)
```

NewAppender creates a new Appender for the specified catalog, schema, and table. If catalog is empty, "memory" is used as the default. If schema is empty, "main" is used as the default. Returns ErrorTypeCatalog if the table does not exist.

<a name="NewAppenderFromConn"></a>
### func [NewAppenderFromConn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L57-L60>)

```go
func NewAppenderFromConn(conn *Conn, schema, table string) (*Appender, error)
```

NewAppenderFromConn creates a new Appender for the specified table. Uses "memory" as the catalog and the provided schema. The schema parameter specifies the schema name \(use "main" for default schema\). Returns ErrorTypeCatalog if the table does not exist.

<a name="NewAppenderWithThreshold"></a>
### func [NewAppenderWithThreshold](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L85-L89>)

```go
func NewAppenderWithThreshold(conn *Conn, catalog, schema, table string, threshold int) (*Appender, error)
```

NewAppenderWithThreshold creates a new Appender with a custom auto\-flush threshold. The threshold must be \>= 1. When the buffer reaches this size, it automatically flushes. Returns ErrorTypeInvalid if threshold \< 1. Returns ErrorTypeCatalog if the table does not exist.

<a name="NewQueryAppender"></a>
### func [NewQueryAppender](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L167-L172>)

```go
func NewQueryAppender(driverConn driver.Conn, query, table string, colTypes []TypeInfo, colNames []string) (*Appender, error)
```

NewQueryAppender creates a query Appender that executes a custom query with batched rows. The batched rows are treated as a temporary table.

Parameters:

- driverConn: The database connection \(must be a \*Conn\)
- query: The SQL query to execute \(INSERT, DELETE, UPDATE, or MERGE INTO\)
- table: The name of the temporary table for batched data \(defaults to "appended\_data" if empty\)
- colTypes: The column types for the temporary table
- colNames: The column names for the temporary table \(defaults to "col1", "col2", ... if empty\)

Returns ErrorTypeInvalid if:

- query is empty
- colTypes is empty
- colNames length doesn't match colTypes length \(when colNames is not empty\)

<a name="NewQueryAppenderWithThreshold"></a>
### func [NewQueryAppenderWithThreshold](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L185-L191>)

```go
func NewQueryAppenderWithThreshold(driverConn driver.Conn, query, table string, colTypes []TypeInfo, colNames []string, threshold int) (*Appender, error)
```

NewQueryAppenderWithThreshold creates a query Appender with a custom auto\-flush threshold. See NewQueryAppender for parameter documentation.

<a name="Appender.AppendRow"></a>
### func \(\*Appender\) [AppendRow](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L515-L517>)

```go
func (a *Appender) AppendRow(values ...any) error
```

AppendRow adds a row to the buffer. If the buffer reaches the threshold, it automatically flushes the data. Returns ErrorTypeClosed if the appender is closed. Returns ErrorTypeInvalid if the number of values doesn't match the column count.

<a name="Appender.BufferSize"></a>
### func \(\*Appender\) [BufferSize](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L1110>)

```go
func (a *Appender) BufferSize() int
```

BufferSize returns the current number of rows in the buffer.

<a name="Appender.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Appender\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L840>)

```go
func (a *Appender) Close() error
```

Close flushes any remaining buffered data and marks the appender as closed. After Close, no more rows can be appended. Returns ErrorTypeClosed if already closed.

<a name="Appender.ColumnTypes"></a>
### func \(\*Appender\) [ColumnTypes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L1105>)


ColumnTypes returns the column types for the table.


<a name="Appender.Flush"></a>
### func \(\*Appender\) [Flush](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L599>)

```go
func (a *Appender) Flush() error
```

Flush writes all buffered rows to the database. On error, the buffer is preserved for retry. Returns ErrorTypeClosed if the appender is closed.

<a name="Appender.FlushWithContext"></a>
### func \(\*Appender\) [FlushWithContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/appender.go#L898-L900>)

```go
func (a *Appender) FlushWithContext(appCtx AppenderContext) error
```

FlushWithContext writes all buffered rows to the database with deadline checking. Uses the injected clock for deadline comparison to enable deterministic testing. On error, the buffer is preserved for retry. Returns context.DeadlineExceeded if the deadline has passed. Returns ErrorTypeClosed if the appender is closed.

```go
    alloc memory.Allocator
}
```

RegisterView registers an Arrow RecordReader as a queryable view. The view can be queried with SQL until the returned release function is called. The caller must call the release function when done to free Arrow resources.

<a name="Backend"></a>
## type [Backend](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L10-L20>)

Backend defines the interface for a DuckDB backend implementation. A backend is responsible for managing connections to the database.

```go
type Backend interface {
    // Open opens a new connection to the database at the given path.
    // The config parameter provides configuration options for the connection.
    Open(
        path string,
        config *Config,
    ) (BackendConn, error)

    // Close closes the backend and releases any associated resources.
    Close() error
}
```

<a name="defaultBackend"></a>defaultBackend holds the registered backend implementation.

```go
var defaultBackend Backend
```

<a name="GetBackend"></a>
### func [GetBackend](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L333>)

```go
func GetBackend() Backend
```

GetBackend returns the registered backend implementation.

<a name="BackendConn"></a>
## type [BackendConn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L24-L67>)

BackendConn represents a connection to a DuckDB database. It provides methods for executing queries and managing the connection.

```go
type BackendConn interface {
    // Execute executes a query that doesn't return rows (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, etc.)
    // and returns the number of rows affected.
    Execute(
        ctx context.Context,
        query string,
        args []driver.NamedValue,
    ) (int64, error)

    // Query executes a query that returns rows (SELECT, etc.) and returns
    // the results as a slice of maps, along with the column names.
    Query(
        ctx context.Context,
        query string,
        args []driver.NamedValue,
    ) ([]map[string]any, []string, error)

    // Prepare prepares a statement for execution and returns a BackendStmt.
    Prepare(
        ctx context.Context,
        query string,
    ) (BackendStmt, error)

    // Close closes the connection and releases any associated resources.
    Close() error

    // Ping verifies that the connection is still alive.
    Ping(ctx context.Context) error

    // AppendDataChunk appends a DataChunk directly to a table, bypassing SQL parsing.
    // This provides efficient bulk data loading for the Appender.
    // Returns the number of rows appended.
    AppendDataChunk(
        ctx context.Context,
        schema, table string,
        chunk *DataChunk,
    ) (int64, error)

    // GetTableTypeInfos returns the TypeInfo for all columns in a table.
    // This is used by the Appender to create DataChunks with the correct types.
    GetTableTypeInfos(
        schema, table string,
    ) ([]TypeInfo, []string, error)
}
```

<a name="BackendConnCatalog"></a>
## type [BackendConnCatalog](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L71-L76>)

BackendConnCatalog is an optional interface for backends that provide access to the catalog. This is needed for virtual table registration.

```go
type BackendConnCatalog interface {
    BackendConn
    // GetCatalog returns the catalog for this connection.
    // Returns nil if catalog access is not supported.
    GetCatalog() any
}
```

<a name="BackendConnIdentifiable"></a>
## type [BackendConnIdentifiable](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L98-L116>)

BackendConnIdentifiable is an optional interface for backends that support connection identification.

This interface enables the public [ConnId](<#ConnId>) API to retrieve unique connection IDs for debugging, tracing, and connection pool management. Backend implementations that support connection identification should implement this interface on their connection types.

### Implementation Requirements

Backends implementing this interface must guarantee:

- ID uniqueness: Each connection gets a distinct ID
- ID stability: The same connection always returns the same ID
- Never\-reuse: IDs are never recycled within a process lifetime
- Thread\-safety: Both methods must be safe for concurrent use

### Example Implementation

See [internal/engine.EngineConn](<https://pkg.go.dev/internal/engine/#EngineConn>) for a reference implementation that uses atomic counters for ID generation.

```go
type BackendConnIdentifiable interface {
    // ID returns the unique connection ID.
    //
    // The ID is assigned when the connection is created and remains stable
    // throughout the connection's lifetime. IDs are unique within a process
    // and never reused (monotonically increasing).
    //
    // Returns 0 only if the implementation failed to assign an ID, which
    // should not occur in normal operation. The public [ConnId] API treats
    // 0 as an invalid ID returned only on error.
    ID() uint64

    // IsClosed returns whether the connection has been closed.
    //
    // This is used by [ConnId] to return an appropriate error when
    // attempting to get the ID of a closed connection. Implementations
    // should return true after [BackendConn.Close] has been called.
    IsClosed() bool
}
```

<a name="BackendConnTableExtractor"></a>
## type [BackendConnTableExtractor](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L142-L153>)

BackendConnTableExtractor is an optional interface for backends that support extracting table names from SQL queries.

This interface enables the public [GetTableNames](<#GetTableNames>) API to extract table references from SQL queries without executing them. Implementations should parse the query and return all table names referenced in the statement.

### Implementation Requirements

Backends implementing this interface must:

- Parse the query to extract table references
- Return an empty slice \(not nil\) for queries with no table references
- Return an error for queries that cannot be parsed
- Handle the qualified parameter to return qualified \(schema.table\) or unqualified names

### Supported Statement Types

Implementations should support at minimum:

- SELECT \(including JOINs and subqueries\)
- INSERT \(target table and SELECT subquery\)
- UPDATE \(target table and WHERE subqueries\)
- DELETE \(target table and WHERE subqueries\)
- CREATE TABLE \(the table being created\)
- DROP TABLE \(the table being dropped\)

```go
type BackendConnTableExtractor interface {
    // ExtractTableNames parses the query and returns all referenced table names.
    //
    // If qualified is true, returns fully qualified names (e.g., "schema.table").
    // If qualified is false, returns just the table name (e.g., "table").
    //
    // Returns:
    //   - A sorted, deduplicated slice of table names
    //   - An empty slice (not nil) if no tables are referenced
    //   - An error if the query cannot be parsed
    ExtractTableNames(query string, qualified bool) ([]string, error)
}
```

<a name="BackendStmt"></a>
## type [BackendStmt](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L156-L176>)

BackendStmt represents a prepared statement from a backend.

```go
type BackendStmt interface {
    // Execute executes the prepared statement with the given arguments
    // and returns the number of rows affected.
    Execute(
        ctx context.Context,
        args []driver.NamedValue,
    ) (int64, error)

    // Query executes the prepared statement with the given arguments
    // and returns the results as a slice of maps, along with the column names.
    Query(
        ctx context.Context,
        args []driver.NamedValue,
    ) ([]map[string]any, []string, error)

    // Close closes the statement and releases any associated resources.
    Close() error

    // NumInput returns the number of placeholder parameters in the statement.
    NumInput() int
}
```

<a name="BackendStmtIntrospector"></a>
## type [BackendStmtIntrospector](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L181-L206>)

BackendStmtIntrospector is an optional interface for backends that support statement introspection. Backends can implement this to provide metadata about prepared statements.

```go
type BackendStmtIntrospector interface {
    BackendStmt

    // StatementType returns the type of the prepared statement.
    StatementType() StmtType

    // ParamName returns the name of the parameter at the given index (1-based).
    // Returns empty string for positional parameters.
    ParamName(index int) string

    // ParamType returns the type of the parameter at the given index (1-based).
    ParamType(index int) Type

    // ColumnCount returns the number of result columns.
    // Returns 0 for non-SELECT statements.
    ColumnCount() int

    // ColumnName returns the name of the result column at the given index (0-based).
    ColumnName(index int) string

    // ColumnType returns the type of the result column at the given index (0-based).
    ColumnType(index int) Type

    // ColumnTypeInfo returns extended type info for the result column at the given index (0-based).
    ColumnTypeInfo(index int) TypeInfo
}
```

<a name="BackendStmtProperties"></a>
## type [BackendStmtProperties](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L317-L321>)

BackendStmtProperties extends BackendStmt with properties access.

```go
type BackendStmtProperties interface {
    BackendStmt
    // Properties returns metadata about the statement's behavior.

}
```

<a name="Bit"></a>
## type [Bit](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L230-L233>)

Bit represents a variable\-length bit string. Bits are stored MSB first in the byte slice.

```go
type Bit struct {
    data   []byte // Bit data (MSB first per byte)
    length int    // Number of valid bits
}
```

<a name="NewBit"></a>
### func [NewBit](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L237>)

```go
func NewBit(bitString string) (Bit, error)
```

NewBit creates a Bit from a bit string \(e.g., "10110"\). Only '0' and '1' characters are allowed.

<a name="NewBitFromBytes"></a>
### func [NewBitFromBytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L270-L273>)

```go
func NewBitFromBytes(data []byte, length int) Bit
```

NewBitFromBytes creates a Bit from bytes with specified length.

<a name="Bit.And"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [And](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L414>)

```go
func (b Bit) And(other Bit) (Bit, error)
```

And returns the bitwise AND of b and other. Both Bits must have the same length.

<a name="Bit.Bytes"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [Bytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L340>)

```go
func (b Bit) Bytes() []byte
```

Bytes returns a copy of the underlying byte slice.

<a name="Bit.Get"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [Get](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L300>)

```go
func (b Bit) Get(pos int) (bool, error)
```

Get returns the bit at position \(0\-indexed\).

<a name="Bit.Len"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [Len](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L335>)

```go
func (b Bit) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of bits.

<a name="Bit.Not"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [Not](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L488>)

```go
func (b Bit) Not() Bit
```

Not returns the bitwise NOT of b.

<a name="Bit.Or"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [Or](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L439>)

```go
func (b Bit) Or(other Bit) (Bit, error)
```

Or returns the bitwise OR of b and other. Both Bits must have the same length.

<a name="Bit.Scan"></a>
### func \(\*Bit\) [Scan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L372>)

```go
func (b *Bit) Scan(v any) error
```

Scan implements the sql.Scanner interface.

<a name="Bit.Set"></a>
### func \(\*Bit\) [Set](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L315>)

```go
func (b *Bit) Set(pos int, val bool) error
```

Set sets the bit at position.

<a name="Bit.String"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L351>)

```go
func (b Bit) String() string
```

String returns bit string representation.

<a name="Bit.Value"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [Value](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L408>)

```go
func (b Bit) Value() (driver.Value, error)
```

Value implements the driver.Valuer interface.

<a name="Bit.Xor"></a>
### func \(Bit\) [Xor](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L464>)

```go
func (b Bit) Xor(other Bit) (Bit, error)
```

Xor returns the bitwise XOR of b and other. Both Bits must have the same length.

<a name="CardinalityInfo"></a>
## type [CardinalityInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L23-L28>)

CardinalityInfo contains the cardinality of a \(table\) function. If it is impossible or difficult to determine the exact cardinality, an approximate cardinality may be used.

```go
type CardinalityInfo struct {
    // Cardinality is the absolute cardinality.
    Cardinality uint
    // Exact indicates whether the cardinality is exact.
    Exact bool
}
```

<a name="ChunkTableFunction"></a>
## type [ChunkTableFunction](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L144>)

ChunkTableFunction is a type which can be bound to return a ChunkTableSource.

```go
type ChunkTableFunction = tableFunction[ChunkTableSource]
```

<a name="ChunkTableSource"></a>
## type [ChunkTableSource](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L101-L108>)

ChunkTableSource represents anything that produces rows in a vectorised way. The cardinality is requested before function initialization. After initializing the ChunkTableSource, the engine requests the rows. It sequentially calls the FillChunk method with a single thread.

```go
type ChunkTableSource interface {
    sequentialTableSource
    // FillChunk takes a DataChunk and fills it with values.
    // Set the chunk size to 0 to end the function.
    // Note: Unlike duckdb-go which uses CGO value semantics, we use pointer
    // for proper Go semantics.
    FillChunk(*DataChunk) error
}
```

<a name="ColumnInfo"></a>
## type [ColumnInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L14-L19>)

ColumnInfo contains the metadata of a column for table functions.

```go
type ColumnInfo struct {
    // Name is the column name.
    Name string
    // T is the column type.
    T   TypeInfo
}
```

<a name="CombineFn"></a>
## type [CombineFn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/aggregate_udf.go#L57>)

CombineFn merges source state into target state \(for parallel execution\).

```go
type CombineFn func(target, source AggregateFuncState) error
```

<a name="Composite"></a>
## type [Composite](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L191-L193>)

Composite can be used as the Scanner type for any composite types \(maps, lists, structs\). It uses mapstructure to decode the value into the target type T.

```go
type Composite[T any] struct {
    t T
}
```

<a name="Composite[T].Get"></a>
### func \(Composite\[T\]\) [Get](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L196>)

```go
func (s Composite[T]) Get() T
```

Get returns the underlying value.

<a name="Composite[T].Scan"></a>
### func \(\*Composite\[T\]\) [Scan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L203>)

```go
func (s *Composite[T]) Scan(v any) error
```

Scan implements the sql.Scanner interface. Uses mapstructure to decode the value into the target type T. NULL values should be handled by the caller.

<a name="Config"></a>
## type [Config](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L209-L248>)

Config holds configuration options for a DuckDB connection.

```go
type Config struct {
    // Path specifies the database path.
    // Use ":memory:" for an in-memory database or a file path for persistent storage.
    Path string

    // AccessMode specifies the access mode for the database.
    // Valid values are "automatic", "read_only", and "read_write".
    // Default is "automatic".
    AccessMode string

    // Threads specifies the number of worker threads.
    // 1-128 is an explicit count. Default is runtime.NumCPU().
    Threads int

    // MaxMemory specifies the maximum memory limit for the database.
    // Examples: "1GB", "512MB", "80%"
    // Default is "80%".
    MaxMemory string

    // Format specifies the database file format.
    // Valid values are "duckdb" (native format) and "parquet" (column-oriented).
    // Default is "duckdb".
    Format string

    // MaxFilesPerGlob specifies the maximum number of files that a glob pattern can match.
    // This limit prevents memory exhaustion when glob patterns match too many files.
    // Default is 10000. Set to 0 to use the default.
    MaxFilesPerGlob int

    // FileGlobTimeout specifies the timeout for cloud storage glob operations.
    // This is useful for preventing long-running list operations on large buckets.
    // Default is 60 seconds. Set to 0 to use the default.
    FileGlobTimeout int

    // CheckpointThreshold specifies the threshold for automatic checkpoint triggering.
    // When the WAL size exceeds this threshold, an automatic checkpoint is triggered.
    // Examples: "256MB", "1GB", "512KB"
    // Default is "256MB".
    CheckpointThreshold string
}
```

<a name="NewConfig"></a>
### func [NewConfig](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/config.go#L133>)

```go
func NewConfig() *Config
```

NewConfig creates a new Config with default values. Defaults:

- Path: "" \(empty, will be set by ParseDSN\)
- AccessMode: "automatic"
- Threads: runtime.NumCPU\(\)
- MaxMemory: "80%"
- MaxFilesPerGlob: DefaultMaxFilesPerGlob \(10000\)
- FileGlobTimeout: DefaultFileGlobTimeout \(60 seconds\)
- CheckpointThreshold: "256MB"

<a name="ParseDSN"></a>
### func [ParseDSN](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/config.go#L68>)

```go
func ParseDSN(dsn string) (*Config, error)
```

ParseDSN parses a DSN string and returns a Config. The DSN format is: path?option=value&option2=value2

Special path values:

- ":memory:" or empty string: in\-memory database
- File path: persistent database \(e.g., "/path/to/db.duckdb"\)

Supported options:

- access\_mode: "automatic", "read\_only", "read\_write" \(default: "automatic"\)
- threads: number of threads, 1\-128 \(default: runtime.NumCPU\(\)\)
- max\_memory: memory limit, e.g., "4GB", "1024MB", "80%" \(default: "80%"\)

Returns an error with ErrorTypeSettings for unknown options.

<a name="Conn"></a>
## type [Conn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L18-L35>)

Conn implements the database/sql/driver connection interfaces. It wraps a BackendConn to provide database/sql compatibility.

```go
type Conn struct {
    connector   *Connector
    backendConn BackendConn
    closed      bool
    tx          bool

    // scalarFuncs holds registered scalar UDFs for this connection.
    scalarFuncs *scalarFuncRegistry

    // aggregateFuncs holds registered aggregate UDFs for this connection.
    aggregateFuncs *aggregateFuncRegistry

    // tableUDFs holds registered table UDFs for this connection.
    tableUDFs *tableFunctionRegistry

    // profiling holds the profiling context for this connection.
    profiling *ProfilingContext
}
```

<a name="Conn.Begin"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [Begin](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L85>)

```go
func (c *Conn) Begin() (driver.Tx, error)
```

Begin starts and returns a new transaction. Deprecated: Use BeginTx instead.

<a name="Conn.BeginTx"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [BeginTx](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L94-L97>)

```go
func (c *Conn) BeginTx(ctx context.Context, opts driver.TxOptions) (driver.Tx, error)
```

BeginTx starts and returns a new transaction. Implements the driver.ConnBeginTx interface.

<a name="Conn.CheckNamedValue"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [CheckNamedValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L203-L205>)

```go
func (c *Conn) CheckNamedValue(nv *driver.NamedValue) error
```

CheckNamedValue implements the driver.NamedValueChecker interface.

<a name="Conn.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L74>)

```go
func (c *Conn) Close() error
```

Close closes the connection. Implements the driver.Conn interface.

<a name="Conn.ExecContext"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [ExecContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L138-L142>)

```go
func (c *Conn) ExecContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args []driver.NamedValue) (driver.Result, error)
```

ExecContext executes a query that doesn't return rows. Implements the driver.ExecerContext interface.

<a name="Conn.IsValid"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [IsValid](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L262>)

```go
func (c *Conn) IsValid() bool
```

IsValid reports whether the connection is still valid. This is called by the connection pool to check if the connection should be discarded. Implements the driver.Validator interface.

<a name="Conn.Ping"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [Ping](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L227>)

```go
func (c *Conn) Ping(ctx context.Context) error
```

Ping verifies the connection is still alive. Implements the driver.Pinger interface.

<a name="Conn.Prepare"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [Prepare](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L39-L41>)

```go
func (c *Conn) Prepare(query string) (driver.Stmt, error)
```

Prepare returns a prepared statement, bound to this connection. Implements the driver.Conn interface.

<a name="Conn.PrepareContext"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [PrepareContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L50-L53>)

```go
func (c *Conn) PrepareContext(ctx context.Context, query string) (driver.Stmt, error)
```

PrepareContext returns a prepared statement, bound to this connection. Implements the driver.ConnPrepareContext interface.

<a name="Conn.PreparePreparedStmt"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [PreparePreparedStmt](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L146-L148>)

```go
func (c *Conn) PreparePreparedStmt(query string) (*PreparedStmt, error)
```

Prepare creates a new prepared statement bound to this connection. The query string may contain $1, $2, etc. for positional parameters or @name for named parameters, but not both styles mixed.

This is a client\-side prepared statement \- the query is stored locally and parameters are bound at execution time.

<a name="Conn.QueryContext"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [QueryContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L170-L174>)

```go
func (c *Conn) QueryContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args []driver.NamedValue) (driver.Rows, error)
```

QueryContext executes a query that returns rows. Implements the driver.QueryerContext interface.

<a name="Conn.ResetSession"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [ResetSession](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L239-L241>)

```go
func (c *Conn) ResetSession(ctx context.Context) error
```

ResetSession resets the session state. This is called by the connection pool before returning the connection to the pool. If a transaction is active, it will be rolled back. Implements the driver.SessionResetter interface.

<a name="Conn.getVirtualTableRegistry"></a>
### func \(\*Conn\) [getVirtualTableRegistry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L268>)

```go
func (c *Conn) getVirtualTableRegistry() VirtualTableRegistry
```

getVirtualTableRegistry returns the virtual table registry from the backend connection if supported. Returns nil if the backend does not support virtual table registration.

<a name="Connector"></a>
## type [Connector](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connector.go#L13-L24>)

Connector implements the driver.Connector interface. It holds the database configuration and can create new connections. The backend is initialized lazily on the first Connect call using sync.Once.

```go
type Connector struct {
    config           *Config
    driver           *Driver
    backend          Backend
    backendConn      BackendConn
    connInitFn       func(driver.ExecerContext) error
    once             sync.Once
    initErr          error
    closed           bool
    mu               sync.RWMutex
    replacementScans *replacementScanRegistry
}
```

<a name="NewConnector"></a>
### func [NewConnector](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connector.go#L31-L34>)

```go
func NewConnector(dsn string, connInitFn func(driver.ExecerContext) error) (*Connector, error)
```

NewConnector creates a new Connector for a DuckDB database. The dsn parameter specifies the database location \(":memory:" for in\-memory, or a file path for persistent storage\). The connInitFn callback is called after each connection is established to perform any additional initialization.

<a name="NewConnectorWithConfig"></a>
### func [NewConnectorWithConfig](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connector.go#L53-L56>)

```go
func NewConnectorWithConfig(config *Config, connInitFn func(driver.ExecerContext) error) (*Connector, error)
```

NewConnectorWithConfig creates a new Connector with the given configuration. This allows for direct configuration without DSN parsing.

<a name="Connector.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Connector\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connector.go#L141>)

```go
func (c *Connector) Close() error
```

Close closes the Connector and releases any associated resources.

<a name="Connector.Config"></a>
### func \(\*Connector\) [Config](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connector.go#L158>)

```go
func (c *Connector) Config() *Config
```

Config returns the configuration used by this connector.

<a name="Connector.Connect"></a>
### func \(\*Connector\) [Connect](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connector.go#L79-L81>)

```go
func (c *Connector) Connect(ctx context.Context) (driver.Conn, error)
```

Connect returns a new connection to the database. The backend is initialized lazily on the first call. The context parameter can be used for connection timeout. Implements driver.Connector.

<a name="Connector.Driver"></a>
### func \(\*Connector\) [Driver](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/connector.go#L71>)

```go
func (c *Connector) Driver() driver.Driver
```

Driver returns the underlying Driver of the Connector. Implements driver.Connector.

<a name="DataChunk"></a>
## type [DataChunk](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L10-L26>)

DataChunk storage of a DuckDB table. A DataChunk contains multiple column vectors and supports vectorized operations.

```go
type DataChunk struct {
    // columns is a helper slice providing direct access to all columns.
    columns []vector

    // columnNames holds the column names, if known.
    columnNames []string

    // size is the current number of valid rows.
    size int

    // projection mapping of projected columns, when known (otherwise nil).
    // If projection[i] == -1, the column is not projected.
    projection []int

    // closed indicates if the chunk has been closed.
    closed bool
}
```

<a name="NewDataChunk"></a>
### func [NewDataChunk](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L263-L265>)

```go
func NewDataChunk(types []TypeInfo) (*DataChunk, error)
```

NewDataChunk creates a new DataChunk from type specifications.

<a name="NewDataChunkWithProjection"></a>
### func [NewDataChunkWithProjection](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L277-L280>)

```go
func NewDataChunkWithProjection(types []TypeInfo, projection []int) (*DataChunk, error)
```

NewDataChunkWithProjection creates a new DataChunk with column projection. projection maps logical column indices to physical column indices. A value of \-1 in projection indicates an unprojected column.

<a name="DataChunk.Close"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L255>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) Close()
```

Close closes all column vectors and the chunk itself.

<a name="DataChunk.GetColumnCount"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [GetColumnCount](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L169>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) GetColumnCount() int
```

GetColumnCount returns the number of columns in the chunk.

<a name="DataChunk.GetSize"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [GetSize](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L35>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) GetSize() int
```

GetSize returns the internal size of the data chunk \(number of valid rows\).

<a name="DataChunk.GetValue"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [GetValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L62-L64>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) GetValue(colIdx, rowIdx int) (any, error)
```

GetValue returns a single value of a column.

<a name="DataChunk.SetSize"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [SetSize](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L40>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) SetSize(size int) error
```

SetSize sets the internal size of the data chunk. Cannot exceed GetDataChunkCapacity\(\).

<a name="DataChunk.SetValue"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [SetValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L95-L98>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) SetValue(colIdx, rowIdx int, val any) error
```

SetValue writes a single value to a column in a data chunk. Note that this requires casting the type for each invocation. If the column is not projected, the value is ignored. NOTE: Custom ENUM types must be passed as string.

<a name="DataChunk.close"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L247>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) close()
```

close releases resources held by the chunk.

<a name="DataChunk.initFromTypes"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [initFromTypes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L219-L221>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) initFromTypes(types []TypeInfo) error
```

initFromTypes initializes the chunk from type specifications.

<a name="DataChunk.reset"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [reset](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L239>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) reset()
```

reset resets the chunk for reuse, preserving the column structure.

<a name="DataChunk.verifyAndRewriteColIdx"></a>
### func \(\*DataChunk\) [verifyAndRewriteColIdx](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/data_chunk.go#L184-L186>)

```go
func (chunk *DataChunk) verifyAndRewriteColIdx(colIdx int) (int, error)
```

verifyAndRewriteColIdx checks whether the provided column index is valid and rewrites it through the projection if one exists.

<a name="Decimal"></a>
## type [Decimal](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L110-L114>)

Decimal represents a DuckDB DECIMAL type with fixed precision and scale. Width is the total number of digits \(precision\). Scale is the number of digits after the decimal point. Value is the unscaled integer value.

```go
type Decimal struct {
    Width uint8
    Scale uint8
    Value *big.Int
}
```

<a name="NewDecimalFromFloat"></a>
### func [NewDecimalFromFloat](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/convert.go#L417>)

```go
func NewDecimalFromFloat(f float64) Decimal
```

NewDecimalFromFloat creates a Decimal from a float64. Uses default width=18 and auto\-detects scale from significant digits.

<a name="ParseDecimal"></a>
### func [ParseDecimal](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/convert.go#L434>)

```go
func ParseDecimal(s string) (Decimal, error)
```

ParseDecimal parses a decimal string like "123.45" into a Decimal.

<a name="convertToDecimal"></a>
### func [convertToDecimal](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/convert.go#L388>)

```go
func convertToDecimal(src any) (Decimal, error)
```

convertToDecimal converts to Decimal type.

<a name="parseDecimal"></a>
### func [parseDecimal](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scan.go#L974>)

```go
func parseDecimal(s string) (Decimal, error)
```

parseDecimal parses a decimal string like "123.45" into a Decimal.

<a name="Decimal.Float64"></a>
### func \(Decimal\) [Float64](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L118>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Float64() float64
```

Float64 converts the Decimal to a float64. Note: This may lose precision for large decimals.

<a name="Decimal.String"></a>
### func \(Decimal\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L135>)

```go
func (d Decimal) String() string
```

String returns the decimal as a formatted string. Trailing zeros are trimmed for cleaner output.

<a name="DecimalDetails"></a>
## type [DecimalDetails](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L57-L60>)

DecimalDetails provides DECIMAL type information.

```go
type DecimalDetails struct {
    Width uint8
    Scale uint8
}
```

<a name="DecimalDetails.isTypeDetails"></a>
### func \(\*DecimalDetails\) [isTypeDetails](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L62>)

```go
func (d *DecimalDetails) isTypeDetails()
```



<a name="Driver"></a>
## type [Driver](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/driver.go#L15>)

Driver implements the database/sql/driver.Driver and driver.DriverContext interfaces. It is stateless and safe for concurrent use.

```go
type Driver struct{}
```

<a name="Driver.Open"></a>
### func \(\*Driver\) [Open](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/driver.go#L28-L30>)

```go
func (d *Driver) Open(dsn string) (driver.Conn, error)
```

Open returns a new connection to the database. The name is a connection string \(DSN\), e.g., ":memory:" for an in\-memory database or a file path for a persistent database.

DSN format: path?option=value&option2=value2 Supported options:

- access\_mode: "automatic", "read\_only", "read\_write" \(default: "automatic"\)
- threads: number of threads, 1\-128 \(default: runtime.NumCPU\(\)\)
- max\_memory: memory limit, e.g., "4GB", "1024MB", "80%" \(default: "80%"\)

Implements driver.Driver.

<a name="Driver.OpenConnector"></a>
### func \(\*Driver\) [OpenConnector](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/driver.go#L42-L44>)

```go
func (d *Driver) OpenConnector(dsn string) (driver.Connector, error)
```

OpenConnector returns a new Connector for the given DSN. The Connector can be used with sql.OpenDB for connection pooling. Implements driver.DriverContext.

<a name="EnumDetails"></a>
## type [EnumDetails](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L65-L67>)

EnumDetails provides ENUM type information.

```go
type EnumDetails struct {
    Values []string
}
```

<a name="EnumDetails.isTypeDetails"></a>
### func \(\*EnumDetails\) [isTypeDetails](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L69>)

```go
func (e *EnumDetails) isTypeDetails()
```

<a name="ErrorType"></a>
## type [ErrorType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/errors.go#L10>)

ErrorType represents the type of a DuckDB error.

```go
type ErrorType int
```

<a name="ErrorTypeInvalid"></a>Error type constants matching DuckDB's error classification.

```go
const (
    ErrorTypeInvalid              ErrorType = 0  // Invalid type.
    ErrorTypeOutOfRange           ErrorType = 1  // The type's value is out of range.
    ErrorTypeConversion           ErrorType = 2  // Conversion/casting error.
    ErrorTypeUnknownType          ErrorType = 3  // The type is unknown.
    ErrorTypeDecimal              ErrorType = 4  // Decimal-related error.
    ErrorTypeMismatchType         ErrorType = 5  // Types don't match.
    ErrorTypeDivideByZero         ErrorType = 6  // Division by zero.
    ErrorTypeObjectSize           ErrorType = 7  // Exceeds object size.
    ErrorTypeInvalidType          ErrorType = 8  // Incompatible types.
    ErrorTypeSerialization        ErrorType = 9  // Type serialization error.
    ErrorTypeTransaction          ErrorType = 10 // Transaction conflict.
    ErrorTypeNotImplemented       ErrorType = 11 // Missing functionality.
    ErrorTypeExpression           ErrorType = 12 // Expression error.
    ErrorTypeCatalog              ErrorType = 13 // Catalog error.
    ErrorTypeParser               ErrorType = 14 // Error during parsing.
    ErrorTypePlanner              ErrorType = 15 // Error during planning.
    ErrorTypeScheduler            ErrorType = 16 // Scheduling error.
    ErrorTypeExecutor             ErrorType = 17 // Executor error.
    ErrorTypeConstraint           ErrorType = 18 // Constraint violation.
    ErrorTypeIndex                ErrorType = 19 // Index error.
    ErrorTypeStat                 ErrorType = 20 // Statistics error.
    ErrorTypeConnection           ErrorType = 21 // Connection error.
    ErrorTypeSyntax               ErrorType = 22 // Invalid syntax.
    ErrorTypeSettings             ErrorType = 23 // Settings-related error.
    ErrorTypeBinder               ErrorType = 24 // Binding error.
    ErrorTypeNetwork              ErrorType = 25 // Network error.
    ErrorTypeOptimizer            ErrorType = 26 // Optimizer error.
    ErrorTypeNullPointer          ErrorType = 27 // Null-pointer exception.
    ErrorTypeIO                   ErrorType = 28 // IO exception.
    ErrorTypeInterrupt            ErrorType = 29 // Query interruption.
    ErrorTypeFatal                ErrorType = 30 // Fatal exception. Non-recoverable.
    ErrorTypeInternal             ErrorType = 31 // Internal exception. Indicates a bug.
    ErrorTypeInvalidInput         ErrorType = 32 // Invalid input.
    ErrorTypeOutOfMemory          ErrorType = 33 // Out-of-memory error.
    ErrorTypePermission           ErrorType = 34 // Invalid permissions.
    ErrorTypeParameterNotResolved ErrorType = 35 // Error when resolving types.
    ErrorTypeParameterNotAllowed  ErrorType = 36 // Invalid parameter.
    ErrorTypeDependency           ErrorType = 37 // Dependency error.
    ErrorTypeHTTP                 ErrorType = 38 // HTTP error.
    ErrorTypeMissingExtension     ErrorType = 39 // Usage of a non-loaded extension.
    ErrorTypeAutoLoad             ErrorType = 40 // Usage of a non-loaded extension that cannot be loaded automatically.
    ErrorTypeSequence             ErrorType = 41 // Sequence error.
    ErrorTypeInvalidConfiguration ErrorType = 42 // Invalid configuration error.
    ErrorTypeClosed               ErrorType = 43 // Resource is closed.
    ErrorTypeBadState             ErrorType = 44 // Invalid state for operation.
)
```
