<a name="ParallelChunkTableFunction"></a>
## type [ParallelChunkTableFunction](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L147>)

ParallelChunkTableFunction is a type which can be bound to return a ParallelChunkTableSource.

```go
type ParallelChunkTableFunction = tableFunction[ParallelChunkTableSource]
```

<a name="wrapChunkTF"></a>
### func [wrapChunkTF](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L257-L259>)

```go
func wrapChunkTF(f ChunkTableFunction) ParallelChunkTableFunction
```

wrapChunkTF wraps a ChunkTableFunction to a ParallelChunkTableFunction.

<a name="ParallelChunkTableSource"></a>
## type [ParallelChunkTableSource](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L116-L125>)

ParallelChunkTableSource represents anything that produces rows in a vectorised way. The cardinality is requested before function initialization. After initializing the ParallelChunkTableSource, the engine requests the rows. It simultaneously calls the FillChunk method with multiple threads. If ParallelTableSourceInfo.MaxThreads is greater than one, FillChunk must use synchronization primitives to avoid race conditions.

```go
type ParallelChunkTableSource interface {
    parallelTableSource
    // FillChunk takes a DataChunk and fills it with values.
    // The localState parameter is passed first (thread-local state from NewLocalState).
    // Set the chunk size to 0 to end the function.
    FillChunk(
        localState any,
        chunk *DataChunk,
    ) error
}
```

<a name="ParallelRowTableFunction"></a>
## type [ParallelRowTableFunction](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L141>)

ParallelRowTableFunction is a type which can be bound to return a ParallelRowTableSource.

```go
type ParallelRowTableFunction = tableFunction[ParallelRowTableSource]
```

<a name="wrapRowTF"></a>
### func [wrapRowTF](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L215-L217>)

```go
func wrapRowTF(f RowTableFunction) ParallelRowTableFunction
```

wrapRowTF wraps a RowTableFunction to a ParallelRowTableFunction.

<a name="ParallelRowTableSource"></a>
## type [ParallelRowTableSource](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L89-L95>)

ParallelRowTableSource represents anything that produces rows in a non\-vectorised way. The cardinality is requested before function initialization. After initializing the ParallelRowTableSource, the engine requests the rows. It simultaneously calls the FillRow method with multiple threads. If ParallelTableSourceInfo.MaxThreads is greater than one, FillRow must use synchronisation primitives to avoid race conditions.

```go
type ParallelRowTableSource interface {
    parallelTableSource
    // FillRow takes a Row and fills it with values.
    // The localState parameter is passed first (thread-local state from NewLocalState).
    // Returns true if there are more rows to fill.
    FillRow(localState any, row Row) (bool, error)
}
```

<a name="ParallelTableSourceInfo"></a>
## type [ParallelTableSourceInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L39-L43>)

ParallelTableSourceInfo contains information for initializing a parallelism\-aware table source.

```go
type ParallelTableSourceInfo struct {
    // MaxThreads is the maximum number of threads on which to run the table source function.
    // If set to 0, it uses the default thread configuration.
    MaxThreads int
}
```

<a name="PreparedStmt"></a>
## type [PreparedStmt](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L18-L27>)

PreparedStmt implements client\-side prepared statement functionality. Since DuckDB CLI doesn't support server\-side prepared statements, this is a client\-side implementation that stores the query and validates parameter counts before delegating to the connection's ExecContext/QueryContext methods which handle parameter binding.

PreparedStmt is safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines.

```go
type PreparedStmt struct {
    conn            *Conn
    query           string
    numParams       int
    stmtType        StmtType
    extractedParams []placeholder // extracted parameters in order
    boundParams     []driver.NamedValue
    closed          bool
    mu              sync.Mutex
}
```

<a name="PreparedStmt.Bind"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [Bind](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L248-L251>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) Bind(idx int, value any) error
```

Bind stores a parameter value for later execution with ExecBound\(\) or QueryBound\(\). The index is 0\-based and must be in range \[0, ParamCount\(\)\). For positional parameters \($1, $2\), idx 0 corresponds to $1, idx 1 to $2, etc. For named parameters \(@name\), idx corresponds to the order of first occurrence.

Bind is not thread\-safe \- do not call concurrently with other Bind calls or with ExecBound/QueryBound.

<a name="PreparedStmt.ClearBindings"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [ClearBindings](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L298>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) ClearBindings()
```

ClearBindings resets all bound parameter values. After calling ClearBindings, you must call Bind for each parameter before calling ExecBound or QueryBound.

<a name="PreparedStmt.Close"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L182>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) Close() error
```

Close closes the prepared statement. Close is idempotent \- calling it multiple times is safe and will not return an error. After Close is called, any attempt to use the statement will return an error.

<a name="PreparedStmt.Exec"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [Exec](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L505-L507>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) Exec(args []driver.Value) (driver.Result, error)
```

Exec executes the prepared statement with the given arguments. Deprecated: Use ExecContext instead.

<a name="PreparedStmt.ExecBound"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [ExecBound](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L321-L323>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) ExecBound(ctx context.Context) (driver.Result, error)
```

ExecBound executes the prepared statement using previously bound parameters. All parameters must be bound using Bind\(\) before calling this method. Returns an error if any parameter has not been bound.

<a name="PreparedStmt.ExecContext"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [ExecContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L415-L418>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) ExecContext(ctx context.Context, args []driver.NamedValue) (driver.Result, error)
```

ExecContext executes the prepared statement with the given arguments. It returns a driver.Result summarizing the effect of the statement.

The args slice must contain exactly NumInput\(\) arguments, with either: \- Positional args: Ordinal field set to 1, 2, 3, etc. \- Named args: Name field set to the parameter name \(without @\)

<a name="PreparedStmt.NumInput"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [NumInput](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L198>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) NumInput() int
```

NumInput returns the number of placeholder parameters in the query. This value is determined at Prepare time and does not change.

For positional placeholders \($1, $2, etc.\), this returns the maximum index. For example, "SELECT $1, $3" returns 3 \(not 2\).

For named placeholders \(@name\), this returns the count of unique names. For example, "SELECT @foo, @foo, @bar" returns 2.

<a name="PreparedStmt.ParamCount"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [ParamCount](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L216>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) ParamCount() int
```

ParamCount returns the number of placeholder parameters in the query. This is an alias for NumInput\(\) for consistency with the introspection API.

<a name="PreparedStmt.ParamName"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [ParamName](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L224-L226>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) ParamName(idx int) (string, error)
```

ParamName returns the name of the parameter at the given index \(0\-based\). For positional parameters \($1, $2\), returns "1", "2", etc. For named parameters \(@name\), returns the parameter name without the @ prefix. Returns an error if the index is out of bounds.

<a name="PreparedStmt.Query"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [Query](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L516-L518>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) Query(args []driver.Value) (driver.Rows, error)
```

Query executes the prepared statement with the given arguments. Deprecated: Use QueryContext instead.

<a name="PreparedStmt.QueryBound"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [QueryBound](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L367-L369>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) QueryBound(ctx context.Context) (driver.Rows, error)
```

QueryBound executes the prepared statement using previously bound parameters and returns the query results as driver.Rows. All parameters must be bound using Bind\(\) before calling this method. Returns an error if any parameter has not been bound.

<a name="PreparedStmt.QueryContext"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [QueryContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L462-L465>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) QueryContext(ctx context.Context, args []driver.NamedValue) (driver.Rows, error)
```

QueryContext executes the prepared statement with the given arguments and returns the query results as driver.Rows.

The args slice must contain exactly NumInput\(\) arguments, with either: \- Positional args: Ordinal field set to 1, 2, 3, etc. \- Named args: Name field set to the parameter name \(without @\)

<a name="PreparedStmt.StatementType"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [StatementType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L209>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) StatementType() StmtType
```

StatementType returns the type of SQL statement \(SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, etc.\). This value is determined at Prepare time using simple keyword detection.

The detection is based on the first keyword in the SQL query after stripping leading comments and whitespace. For complex cases where full parsing is needed, use the parser package directly.

<a name="PreparedStmt.allParamsBound"></a>
### func \(\*PreparedStmt\) [allParamsBound](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L305>)

```go
func (s *PreparedStmt) allParamsBound() bool
```

allParamsBound checks if all parameters have been bound.

ProfilingContext captures timing and metrics with an injected clock for deterministic testing.

```go
type ProfilingContext struct {

}
```


```go
func (p *ProfilingContext) AddOperator(operatorType string, rows int, timing time.Duration)
```



Disable disables profiling.

<a name="ProfilingContext.Elapsed"></a>



<a name="ProfilingInfo"></a>
## type [ProfilingInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/profiling.go#L17-L23>)

ProfilingInfo is a recursive type containing metrics for each node in the query plan. There are two types of nodes: the QUERY\_ROOT and OPERATOR nodes. The QUERY\_ROOT refers exclusively to the top\-level node; its metrics are measured over the entire query. The OPERATOR nodes refer to the individual operators in the query plan.

```go
type ProfilingInfo struct {
    // Metrics contains all key-value pairs of the current node.
    // The key represents the name and corresponds to the measured value.
    Metrics map[string]string
    // Children contains all children of the node and their respective metrics.
    Children []ProfilingInfo
}
```

<a name="GetProfilingInfo"></a>
### func [GetProfilingInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/profiling.go#L191-L193>)

```go
func GetProfilingInfo(c *sql.Conn) (ProfilingInfo, error)
```

GetProfilingInfo obtains all available metrics set by the current connection. Profiling must be enabled via PRAGMA enable\_profiling before executing queries.

<a name="ReplacementScanCallback"></a>
## type [ReplacementScanCallback](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L17>)

ReplacementScanCallback is called when a table reference is encountered during query binding. It returns: \- functionName: the name of the function to replace the table reference with \(empty means no replacement\) \- params: parameters to pass to the replacement function \(only string, int64, \[\]string supported\) \- error: an error if the replacement scan fails

```go
type ReplacementScanCallback func(tableName string) (string, []any, error)
```

<a name="ReplacementScanContext"></a>
## type [ReplacementScanContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L21-L24>)

ReplacementScanContext provides context and clock for replacement scan execution. The clock is used for deterministic deadline checking in tests.

```go
type ReplacementScanContext struct {
    ctx   context.Context
    clock quartz.Clock
}
```

```go
func NewReplacementScanContext(ctx context.Context, clock quartz.Clock) *ReplacementScanContext
```

NewReplacementScanContext creates a new ReplacementScanContext. If clock is nil, the real system clock is used.

<a name="ReplacementScanContext.Clock"></a>
### func \(\*ReplacementScanContext\) [Clock](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L61>)

```go
func (c *ReplacementScanContext) Clock() quartz.Clock
```

Clock returns the clock used for timing.

<a name="ReplacementScanContext.Context"></a>
### func \(\*ReplacementScanContext\) [Context](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L56>)

```go
func (c *ReplacementScanContext) Context() context.Context
```

Context returns the underlying context.

<a name="ReplacementScanContext.WithClock"></a>

```go
func (c *ReplacementScanContext) WithClock(clock quartz.Clock) *ReplacementScanContext
```

WithClock returns a new ReplacementScanContext with the given clock.

<a name="ReplacementScanContext.executeCallback"></a>
### func \(\*ReplacementScanContext\) [executeCallback](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L67-L70>)

```go
func (c *ReplacementScanContext) executeCallback(callback ReplacementScanCallback, tableName string) (string, []any, error)
```

executeCallback executes the replacement scan callback with deadline checking. If the context has a deadline and it has passed \(according to the clock\), returns context.DeadlineExceeded.

<a name="ReplacementScanResult"></a>
## type [ReplacementScanResult](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L149-L159>)

ReplacementScanResult holds the result of a replacement scan attempt.

```go
type ReplacementScanResult struct {
    // FunctionName is the name of the function to replace the table with.
    // Empty means no replacement was made.
    FunctionName string
    // Params are the parameters to pass to the replacement function.
    Params []any
    // Replaced indicates whether a replacement was made.
    Replaced bool
    // Error holds any error that occurred during the scan.
    Error error
}
```

<a name="TryReplacementScan"></a>
### func [TryReplacementScan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L163-L166>)

```go
func TryReplacementScan(c *Connector, tableName string) ReplacementScanResult
```

TryReplacementScan attempts to replace a table reference using the connector's replacement scan callback. This is called by the binder during table resolution.

<a name="TryReplacementScanWithContext"></a>
### func [TryReplacementScanWithContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/replacement_scan.go#L176-L180>)

```go
func TryReplacementScanWithContext(c *Connector, tableName string, ctx *ReplacementScanContext) ReplacementScanResult
```

TryReplacementScanWithContext attempts to replace a table reference using the connector's replacement scan callback with a context for timeout checking.

<a name="Row"></a>
## type [Row](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/row.go#L7-L13>)

Row provides row\-oriented access within a DataChunk. It maintains a reference to the parent chunk and the row index.

```go
type Row struct {
    // chunk is a reference to the parent DataChunk.
    chunk *DataChunk

    // rowIdx is the index of this row within the chunk.
    rowIdx int
}
```

<a name="NewRow"></a>
### func [NewRow](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/row.go#L16>)

```go
func NewRow(chunk *DataChunk, rowIdx int) Row
```

NewRow creates a new Row accessor for a specific row in a DataChunk.

<a name="Row.Chunk"></a>
### func \(Row\) [Chunk](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/row.go#L80>)

```go
func (r Row) Chunk() *DataChunk
```

Chunk returns the parent DataChunk.

<a name="Row.GetRowValue"></a>
### func \(Row\) [GetRowValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/row.go#L62-L64>)

```go
func (r Row) GetRowValue(colIdx int) (any, error)
```

GetRowValue gets a value at the specified column index for this row.

<a name="Row.IsProjected"></a>
### func \(Row\) [IsProjected](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/row.go#L26>)

```go
func (r Row) IsProjected(colIdx int) bool
```

IsProjected returns whether the column at colIdx is projected. Returns true if the column is projected \(accessible\), false otherwise. If the chunk has no projection, all columns are considered projected.

<a name="Row.RowIndex"></a>
### func \(Row\) [RowIndex](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/row.go#L75>)

```go
func (r Row) RowIndex() int
```

RowIndex returns the row index within the parent chunk.

<a name="Row.SetRowValue"></a>
### func \(Row\) [SetRowValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/row.go#L48-L51>)

```go
func (r Row) SetRowValue(colIdx int, val any) error
```

SetRowValue sets a value at the specified column index for this row. If the column is not projected, the value is silently ignored.

<a name="RowContextExecutorFn"></a>
## type [RowContextExecutorFn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L50>)

RowContextExecutorFn accepts a row\-based execution function using a context. It takes a context and the row values, and returns the row execution result, or error.

```go
type RowContextExecutorFn func(ctx context.Context, values []driver.Value) (any, error)
```

<a name="RowExecutorFn"></a>
## type [RowExecutorFn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L47>)

RowExecutorFn is the type for any row\-based execution function. It takes the row values and returns the row execution result, or error.

```go
type RowExecutorFn func(values []driver.Value) (any, error)
```

<a name="RowIterator"></a>
## type [RowIterator](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/virtual_table.go#L34-L47>)

RowIterator iterates over virtual table rows.

```go
type RowIterator interface {
    // Next advances to the next row. Returns false when done.
    Next() bool

    // Values returns the current row values.
    // The returned slice is valid until the next call to Next.
    Values() []any

    // Err returns any error that occurred during iteration.
    Err() error

    // Close releases resources held by the iterator.
    Close() error
}
```

<a name="RowTableFunction"></a>
## type [RowTableFunction](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L138>)

RowTableFunction is a type which can be bound to return a RowTableSource.

```go
type RowTableFunction = tableFunction[RowTableSource]
```

<a name="RowTableSource"></a>
## type [RowTableSource](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L76-L81>)

RowTableSource represents anything that produces rows in a non\-vectorised way. The cardinality is requested before function initialization. After initializing the RowTableSource, the engine requests the rows. It sequentially calls the FillRow method with a single thread.

```go
type RowTableSource interface {
    sequentialTableSource
    // FillRow takes a Row and fills it with values.
    // Returns true if there are more rows to fill.
    FillRow(Row) (bool, error)
}
```

<a name="Rows"></a>
## type [Rows](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L15-L21>)

Rows implements driver.Rows for DuckDB query results. It stores pre\-parsed row data from backend JSON responses. Rows is not thread\-safe and should be used from a single goroutine.

```go
type Rows struct {
    columns  []string // Column names in order
    colTypes []Type   // DuckDB type for each column
    data     [][]any  // Pre-parsed row data
    index    int      // Current row index (-1 before first Next)
    closed   bool     // Closed flag
}
```

<a name="NewRows"></a>
### func [NewRows](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L27-L31>)

```go
func NewRows(columns []string, colTypes []Type, data [][]any) *Rows
```

NewRows creates a new Rows instance from backend response data. columns contains the column names in order. colTypes contains the DuckDB type for each column. data contains the pre\-parsed row data.

<a name="Rows.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L49>)

```go
func (r *Rows) Close() error
```

Close closes the Rows, preventing further iteration. Implements driver.Rows.

<a name="Rows.ColumnTypeDatabaseTypeName"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [ColumnTypeDatabaseTypeName](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L200-L202>)

```go
func (r *Rows) ColumnTypeDatabaseTypeName(index int) string
```

ColumnTypeDatabaseTypeName returns the DuckDB type name for the column at the given index. Implements driver.RowsColumnTypeDatabaseTypeName.

<a name="Rows.ColumnTypeNullable"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [ColumnTypeNullable](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L213-L215>)

```go
func (r *Rows) ColumnTypeNullable(index int) (nullable, ok bool)
```

ColumnTypeNullable returns whether the column at the given index is nullable. All DuckDB columns are nullable, so this always returns \(true, true\). Implements driver.RowsColumnTypeNullable.

<a name="Rows.ColumnTypeScanType"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [ColumnTypeScanType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L124-L126>)

```go
func (r *Rows) ColumnTypeScanType(index int) reflect.Type
```

ColumnTypeScanType returns the Go type suitable for scanning into for the column at the given index. Implements driver.RowsColumnTypeScanType.

<a name="Rows.Columns"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [Columns](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L43>)

```go
func (r *Rows) Columns() []string
```

Columns returns the column names of the result set. Implements driver.Rows.

<a name="Rows.Next"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [Next](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L62>)

```go
func (r *Rows) Next(dest []driver.Value) error
```

Next advances to the next row and populates dest with row values. Returns io.EOF when no more rows are available. Implements driver.Rows.

<a name="Rows.Scan"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [Scan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L85>)

```go
func (r *Rows) Scan(dest ...any) error
```

Scan scans the current row into the provided destination pointers. Each dest must be a pointer to a value that the column can be scanned into. Returns an error if the Rows is closed, no current row exists, or the destination count doesn't match the column count.

<a name="Rows.columnTypes"></a>
### func \(\*Rows\) [columnTypes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/rows.go#L293>)

```go
func (r *Rows) columnTypes() []TypeInfo
```

columnTypes returns TypeInfo for each column. For simple types, this creates a basic TypeInfo wrapper. For complex types \(LIST, STRUCT, etc.\), full type info would need to be stored separately during query execution.

<a name="ScalarBinderFn"></a>
## type [ScalarBinderFn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L53>)

ScalarBinderFn takes a context and the scalar function's arguments. It returns the updated context, which can now contain arbitrary data available during execution.

```go
type ScalarBinderFn func(ctx context.Context, args []ScalarUDFArg) (context.Context, error)
```

<a name="ScalarFunc"></a>
## type [ScalarFunc](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L69-L74>)

ScalarFunc is the user\-defined scalar function interface. Any scalar function must implement a Config function, and an Executor function.

```go
type ScalarFunc interface {
    // Config returns ScalarFuncConfig to configure the scalar function.
    Config() ScalarFuncConfig
    // Executor returns ScalarFuncExecutor to execute the scalar function.
    Executor() ScalarFuncExecutor
}
```

<a name="ScalarFuncConfig"></a>
## type [ScalarFuncConfig](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L14-L34>)

ScalarFuncConfig contains the fields to configure a user\-defined scalar function.

```go
type ScalarFuncConfig struct {
    // InputTypeInfos contains Type information for each input parameter of the scalar function.
    InputTypeInfos []TypeInfo
    // ResultTypeInfo holds the Type information of the scalar function's result type.
    ResultTypeInfo TypeInfo

    // VariadicTypeInfo configures the number of input parameters.
    // If this field is nil, then the input parameters match InputTypeInfos.
    // Otherwise, the scalar function's input parameters are set to variadic, allowing any number of input parameters.
    // The Type of the first len(InputTypeInfos) parameters is configured by InputTypeInfos, and all
    // remaining parameters must match the variadic Type. To configure different variadic parameter types,
    // you must set the VariadicTypeInfo's Type to TYPE_ANY.
    VariadicTypeInfo TypeInfo
    // Volatile sets the stability of the scalar function to volatile, if true.
    // Volatile scalar functions might create a different result per row.
    // E.g., random() is a volatile scalar function.
    Volatile bool
    // SpecialNullHandling disables the default NULL handling of scalar functions, if true.
    // The default NULL handling is: NULL in, NULL out. I.e., if any input parameter is NULL, then the result is NULL.
    SpecialNullHandling bool
}
```

## type [ScalarFuncContext](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L77-L80>)

ScalarFuncContext provides context for UDF execution with deterministic time access.

```go
}

```

### func \(\*ScalarFuncContext\) [checkTimeout](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L118>)


<a name="ScalarFuncExecutor"></a>
## type [ScalarFuncExecutor](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scalar_udf.go#L58-L65>)

ScalarFuncExecutor contains the functions to execute a user\-defined scalar function. It invokes its first non\-nil member.

```go
type ScalarFuncExecutor struct {
    // RowExecutor accepts a row-based execution function of type RowExecutorFn.
    RowExecutor RowExecutorFn
    // RowContextExecutor accepts a row-based execution function of type RowContextExecutorFn.
    RowContextExecutor RowContextExecutorFn
    // ScalarBinder accepts a bind function of type ScalarBinderFn.
    ScalarBinder ScalarBinderFn
}
```

ScalarUDFArg contains scalar UDF argument metadata and the optional argument.

```go
type ScalarUDFArg struct {
    // Foldable is true, if the argument was folded into a value, else false.
    Foldable bool
    // Value is the folded argument value, or nil, if the argument is not foldable.
    Value driver.Value
}
```

```go

```

Close closes the statement.

<a name="Stmt.ColumnCount"></a>
### func \(\*Stmt\) [ColumnCount](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/conn.go#L524>)

```go
func (s *Stmt) ColumnCount() (int, error)
```

<a name="StmtProperties"></a>
## type [StmtProperties](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L307-L314>)

StmtProperties provides metadata about statement behavior.

```go
type StmtProperties struct {
    Type        StmtType       // Statement type (SELECT, INSERT, etc.)
    ReturnType  StmtReturnType // What the statement returns
    IsReadOnly  bool           // True if statement doesn't modify data
    IsStreaming bool           // True if result can be streamed
    ColumnCount int            // Number of result columns (0 for non-query)
    ParamCount  int            // Number of parameters
}
```

<a name="StmtReturnType"></a>
## type [StmtReturnType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L293>)

StmtReturnType indicates what a statement returns when executed.

```go
type StmtReturnType uint8
```

<a name="RETURN_QUERY_RESULT"></a>

```go
const (
    // RETURN_QUERY_RESULT - Statement returns rows (SELECT, PRAGMA, SHOW, EXPLAIN)
    RETURN_QUERY_RESULT StmtReturnType = iota

    // RETURN_CHANGED_ROWS - Statement returns affected row count (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
    RETURN_CHANGED_ROWS

    // RETURN_NOTHING - Statement returns nothing (DDL, SET, ATTACH, etc.)
    RETURN_NOTHING
)
```

<a name="StmtReturnType.String"></a>
### func \(StmtReturnType\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/stmt_type.go#L149>)

```go
func (t StmtReturnType) String() string
```

String returns the string representation of a return type.

<a name="StmtType"></a>
## type [StmtType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/backend.go#L253>)

StmtType represents the type of a SQL statement.

```go
type StmtType int
```

<a name="STATEMENT_TYPE_INVALID"></a>Statement type constants.

```go
const (
    STATEMENT_TYPE_INVALID           StmtType = 0
    STATEMENT_TYPE_SELECT            StmtType = 1
    STATEMENT_TYPE_INSERT            StmtType = 2
    STATEMENT_TYPE_UPDATE            StmtType = 3
    STATEMENT_TYPE_EXPLAIN           StmtType = 4
    STATEMENT_TYPE_DELETE            StmtType = 5
    STATEMENT_TYPE_PREPARE           StmtType = 6
    STATEMENT_TYPE_CREATE            StmtType = 7
    STATEMENT_TYPE_EXECUTE           StmtType = 8
    STATEMENT_TYPE_ALTER             StmtType = 9
    STATEMENT_TYPE_TRANSACTION       StmtType = 10
    STATEMENT_TYPE_COPY              StmtType = 11
    STATEMENT_TYPE_ANALYZE           StmtType = 12
    STATEMENT_TYPE_VARIABLE_SET      StmtType = 13
    STATEMENT_TYPE_CREATE_FUNC       StmtType = 14
    STATEMENT_TYPE_DROP              StmtType = 15
    STATEMENT_TYPE_EXPORT            StmtType = 16
    STATEMENT_TYPE_PRAGMA            StmtType = 17
    STATEMENT_TYPE_VACUUM            StmtType = 18
    STATEMENT_TYPE_CALL              StmtType = 19
    STATEMENT_TYPE_SET               StmtType = 20
    STATEMENT_TYPE_LOAD              StmtType = 21
    STATEMENT_TYPE_RELATION          StmtType = 22
    STATEMENT_TYPE_EXTENSION         StmtType = 23
    STATEMENT_TYPE_LOGICAL_PLAN      StmtType = 24
    STATEMENT_TYPE_ATTACH            StmtType = 25
    STATEMENT_TYPE_DETACH            StmtType = 26
    STATEMENT_TYPE_MULTI             StmtType = 27
    STATEMENT_TYPE_MERGE_INTO        StmtType = 28
    STATEMENT_TYPE_UPDATE_EXTENSIONS StmtType = 29
    STATEMENT_TYPE_COPY_DATABASE     StmtType = 30
    STATEMENT_TYPE_PIVOT             StmtType = 31
    STATEMENT_TYPE_UNPIVOT           StmtType = 32
)
```

<a name="detectStatementType"></a>
### func [detectStatementType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/stmt_detector.go#L16>)

```go
func detectStatementType(sql string) StmtType
```

detectStatementType detects the type of a SQL statement using simple keyword extraction. This is a lightweight function that extracts the first keyword \(after stripping comments and whitespace\) and maps it to a StmtType.

This function handles:

- Leading whitespace
- Single\-line comments \(\-\- and //\)
- Multi\-line comments \(/\* \*/\)
- Case\-insensitive keyword matching

<a name="keywordToStmtType"></a>
### func [keywordToStmtType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/stmt_detector.go#L96>)

```go
func keywordToStmtType(keyword string) StmtType
```

keywordToStmtType maps a SQL keyword to a StmtType.

<a name="StructEntry"></a>

```go
type StructEntry interface {

}

```

<a name="StructScanner"></a>
## type [StructScanner](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scan_types.go#L97-L99>)

StructScanner enables scanning STRUCT columns to Go structs. Field mapping uses the duckdb struct tag for custom names.

```go
type StructScanner[T any] struct {

}
```


<a name="StructValue"></a>
## type [StructValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/bind_types.go#L27-L29>)

StructValue wraps a Go struct for binding as STRUCT parameter. Implements driver.Valuer to convert structs to map\[string\]any. Uses the duckdb struct tag for custom field names.

```go
type StructValue[T any] struct {
    V T
}
```


Value implements the driver.Valuer interface. Converts the struct to map\[string\]any for driver binding.

<a name="TimeNS"></a>
## type [TimeNS](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L518>)

TimeNS represents time with nanosecond precision. Stored as nanoseconds since midnight \(0 to 86399999999999\).

```go
type TimeNS int64
```

<a name="CurrentTimeNS"></a>
### func [CurrentTimeNS](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L660>)

```go
func CurrentTimeNS(clock quartz.Clock) TimeNS
```

CurrentTimeNS returns the current time as TimeNS using the provided clock. This enables deterministic testing by using quartz.Mock instead of real time.

<a name="NewTimeNS"></a>
### func [NewTimeNS](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L521-L524>)

```go
func NewTimeNS(hour, min, sec int, nsec int64) TimeNS
```

NewTimeNS creates TimeNS from hour, minute, second, and nanosecond components.

<a name="NowNS"></a>
### func [NowNS](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L649>)

```go
func NowNS(clock quartz.Clock) TimeNS
```

NowNS returns the current time as TimeNS using the provided clock. This is an alias for CurrentTimeNS, providing a more intuitive name. This enables deterministic testing by using quartz.Mock instead of real time.

<a name="TimeNSFromTime"></a>
### func [TimeNSFromTime](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L564>)

```go
func TimeNSFromTime(t time.Time) TimeNS
```

FromTime creates TimeNS from a time.Time value.

```go
func (t TimeNS) Components() (hour, min, sec int, nsec int64)
```

Components extracts hour, minute, second, and nanosecond from TimeNS.

<a name="TimeNS.Scan"></a>
### func \(\*TimeNS\) [Scan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L595>)

```go
func (t *TimeNS) Scan(v any) error
```

Scan implements the sql.Scanner interface.

<a name="TimeNS.String"></a>
### func \(TimeNS\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L574>)

```go
func (t TimeNS) String() string
```

String returns the time in "HH:MM:SS.nnnnnnnnn" format.

<a name="TimeNS.ToTime"></a>
### func \(TimeNS\) [ToTime](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L548>)

```go
func (t TimeNS) ToTime() time.Time
```

ToTime converts TimeNS to time.Time. The date portion is set to Unix epoch \(1970\-01\-01\).

```go

```

Value implements the driver.Valuer interface.

TimeNSScanner scans TIME with nanosecond precision from database. The scanned value is stored as TimeNS \(nanoseconds since midnight\).

```go
}
```


<a name="Type"></a>
## type [Type](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_enum.go#L5>)

Type represents a DuckDB type enumeration. This is a pure Go implementation that mirrors the DuckDB type system.

```go
type Type uint8
```

<a name="TYPE_INVALID"></a>DuckDB type constants. These values match the DuckDB internal type enumeration \(0\-36\).

```go
const (
    TYPE_INVALID      Type = 0   // Invalid type
    TYPE_BOOLEAN      Type = 1   // Boolean (true/false)
    TYPE_TINYINT      Type = 2   // 8-bit signed integer
    TYPE_SMALLINT     Type = 3   // 16-bit signed integer
    TYPE_INTEGER      Type = 4   // 32-bit signed integer
    TYPE_BIGINT       Type = 5   // 64-bit signed integer
    TYPE_UTINYINT     Type = 6   // 8-bit unsigned integer
    TYPE_USMALLINT    Type = 7   // 16-bit unsigned integer
    TYPE_UINTEGER     Type = 8   // 32-bit unsigned integer
    TYPE_UBIGINT      Type = 9   // 64-bit unsigned integer
    TYPE_FLOAT        Type = 10  // 32-bit floating point
    TYPE_DOUBLE       Type = 11  // 64-bit floating point
    TYPE_TIMESTAMP    Type = 12  // Timestamp (microseconds since epoch)
    TYPE_DATE         Type = 13  // Date (days since epoch)
    TYPE_TIME         Type = 14  // Time (microseconds since midnight)
    TYPE_INTERVAL     Type = 15  // Interval (months, days, microseconds)
    TYPE_HUGEINT      Type = 16  // 128-bit signed integer
    TYPE_UHUGEINT     Type = 17  // 128-bit unsigned integer
    TYPE_VARCHAR      Type = 18  // Variable-length string
    TYPE_BLOB         Type = 19  // Binary large object
    TYPE_DECIMAL      Type = 20  // Fixed-point decimal
    TYPE_TIMESTAMP_S  Type = 21  // Timestamp (seconds precision)
    TYPE_TIMESTAMP_MS Type = 22  // Timestamp (milliseconds precision)
    TYPE_TIMESTAMP_NS Type = 23  // Timestamp (nanoseconds precision)
    TYPE_ENUM         Type = 24  // Enumeration type
    TYPE_LIST         Type = 25  // Variable-length list
    TYPE_STRUCT       Type = 26  // Struct (named fields)
    TYPE_MAP          Type = 27  // Map (key-value pairs)
    TYPE_ARRAY        Type = 28  // Fixed-length array
    TYPE_UUID         Type = 29  // UUID (128-bit)
    TYPE_UNION        Type = 30  // Union type
    TYPE_BIT          Type = 31  // Bit string
    TYPE_TIME_TZ      Type = 32  // Time with time zone
    TYPE_TIMESTAMP_TZ Type = 33  // Timestamp with time zone
    TYPE_ANY          Type = 34  // Any type (for UDFs)
    TYPE_BIGNUM       Type = 35  // Variable-width decimal (internal)
    TYPE_SQLNULL      Type = 36  // SQL NULL type
    TYPE_JSON         Type = 37  // JSON data type
    TYPE_GEOMETRY     Type = 60  // Geometry type (spatial)
    TYPE_LAMBDA       Type = 106 // Lambda function type
    TYPE_VARIANT      Type = 109 // Variant type (dynamic)
)
```

<a name="TypeDetails"></a>
## type [TypeDetails](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L52-L54>)

TypeDetails is an interface for type\-specific details. Use type assertion to access specific detail types.

```go
type TypeDetails interface {
    isTypeDetails()
}
```

<a name="TypeInfo"></a>
## type [TypeInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L149-L159>)

TypeInfo is an interface for a DuckDB type.

```go
type TypeInfo interface {
    // InternalType returns the Type.
    InternalType() Type
    // Details returns type-specific details for complex types.
    // Returns nil for simple/primitive types.
    // Use type assertion to access specific detail types.
    Details() TypeDetails
    // SQLType returns the SQL type string for use in CREATE TABLE statements.
    // For example: "INTEGER", "VARCHAR", "DECIMAL(10,2)", "INTEGER[]", "STRUCT(a INTEGER, b VARCHAR)".
    SQLType() string
}
```

<a name="NewArrayInfo"></a>
### func [NewArrayInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L728-L731>)

```go
func NewArrayInfo(childInfo TypeInfo, size uint64) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewArrayInfo returns ARRAY type information. childInfo contains the type information of the ARRAY's elements. size is the ARRAY's fixed size.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewArrayInfo demonstrates creating fixed\-size ARRAY type information.

```go
// Create a fixed-size array of 3 integers
intInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_INTEGER,
)
arrayInfo, err := dukdb.NewArrayInfo(
	intInfo,
	3,
)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("SQL:", arrayInfo.SQLType())

// Access the array details
details := arrayInfo.Details().(*dukdb.ArrayDetails)
fmt.Println("Size:", details.Size)
fmt.Println(
	"Child type:",
	details.Child.SQLType(),
)

// Output:
// SQL: INTEGER[3]
// Size: 3
// Child type: INTEGER
```

#### Output

```
SQL: INTEGER[3]
Size: 3
Child type: INTEGER
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewBignumInfo"></a>
### func [NewBignumInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L810>)

```go
func NewBignumInfo() (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewBignumInfo returns BIGNUM type information.

<a name="NewDecimalInfo"></a>
### func [NewDecimalInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L552-L554>)

```go
func NewDecimalInfo(width, scale uint8) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewDecimalInfo returns DECIMAL type information. Its input parameters are the width and scale of the DECIMAL type.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewDecimalInfo demonstrates creating DECIMAL type information.

```go
// Create DECIMAL(10,2) for currency values
decInfo, err := dukdb.NewDecimalInfo(10, 2)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("SQL:", decInfo.SQLType())

// Access details
details := decInfo.Details().(*dukdb.DecimalDetails)
fmt.Println("Width:", details.Width)
fmt.Println("Scale:", details.Scale)

// Output:
// SQL: DECIMAL(10,2)
// Width: 10
// Scale: 2
```

#### Output

```
SQL: DECIMAL(10,2)
Width: 10
Scale: 2
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewEnumInfo"></a>
### func [NewEnumInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L577-L580>)

```go
func NewEnumInfo(first string, others ...string) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewEnumInfo returns ENUM type information. Its input parameters are the dictionary values.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewEnumInfo demonstrates creating ENUM type information.

```go
// Create an enum type for t-shirt sizes
enumInfo, err := dukdb.NewEnumInfo(
	"small",
	"medium",
	"large",
	"x-large",
)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("SQL:", enumInfo.SQLType())

// Access the enum values
details := enumInfo.Details().(*dukdb.EnumDetails)
fmt.Println("Values:", details.Values)

// Output:
// SQL: ENUM('small', 'medium', 'large', 'x-large')
// Values: [small medium large x-large]
```

#### Output

```
SQL: ENUM('small', 'medium', 'large', 'x-large')
Values: [small medium large x-large]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewGeometryInfo"></a>
### func [NewGeometryInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L818>)

```go
func NewGeometryInfo() (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewGeometryInfo returns GEOMETRY type information.

<a name="NewJSONInfo"></a>
### func [NewJSONInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L802>)

```go
func NewJSONInfo() (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewJSONInfo returns JSON type information.

<a name="NewLambdaInfo"></a>
### func [NewLambdaInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L836>)

```go
func NewLambdaInfo() (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewLambdaInfo returns LAMBDA type information. LAMBDA is used for higher\-order function support, stored as expression strings.

<a name="NewListInfo"></a>
### func [NewListInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L607-L609>)

```go
func NewListInfo(childInfo TypeInfo) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewListInfo returns LIST type information. childInfo contains the type information of the LIST's elements.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewListInfo demonstrates creating LIST type information.

```go
// Create a list of integers
intInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_INTEGER,
)
listInfo, err := dukdb.NewListInfo(intInfo)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("SQL:", listInfo.SQLType())

// Access the child type
details := listInfo.Details().(*dukdb.ListDetails)
fmt.Println(
	"Child type:",
	details.Child.SQLType(),
)

// Output:
// SQL: INTEGER[]
// Child type: INTEGER
```

#### Output

```
SQL: INTEGER[]
Child type: INTEGER
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewMapInfo"></a>
### func [NewMapInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L703-L705>)

```go
func NewMapInfo(keyInfo, valueInfo TypeInfo) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewMapInfo returns MAP type information. keyInfo contains the type information of the MAP keys. valueInfo contains the type information of the MAP values.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewMapInfo demonstrates creating MAP type information.

```go
// Create a map from string to integer
keyInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_VARCHAR,
)
valueInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_INTEGER,
)

mapInfo, err := dukdb.NewMapInfo(
	keyInfo,
	valueInfo,
)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("SQL:", mapInfo.SQLType())

// Access the key and value types
details := mapInfo.Details().(*dukdb.MapDetails)
fmt.Println(
	"Key type:",
	details.Key.SQLType(),
)
fmt.Println(
	"Value type:",
	details.Value.SQLType(),
)

// Output:
// SQL: MAP(VARCHAR, INTEGER)
// Key type: VARCHAR
// Value type: INTEGER
```

#### Output

```
SQL: MAP(VARCHAR, INTEGER)
Key type: VARCHAR
Value type: INTEGER
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewStructInfo"></a>
### func [NewStructInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L625-L628>)

```go
func NewStructInfo(firstEntry StructEntry, others ...StructEntry) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewStructInfo returns STRUCT type information. Its input parameters are the STRUCT entries.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewStructInfo demonstrates creating STRUCT type information.

```go
// Create struct entries
intInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_INTEGER,
)
strInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_VARCHAR,
)

idEntry, _ := dukdb.NewStructEntry(
	intInfo,
	"id",
)
nameEntry, _ := dukdb.NewStructEntry(
	strInfo,
	"name",
)

// Create the struct type
structInfo, err := dukdb.NewStructInfo(
	idEntry,
	nameEntry,
)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("SQL:", structInfo.SQLType())

// Access struct fields
details := structInfo.Details().(*dukdb.StructDetails)
for _, entry := range details.Entries {
	fmt.Printf(
		"Field %q: %s\n",
		entry.Name(),
		entry.Info().SQLType(),
	)
}

// Output:
// SQL: STRUCT("id" INTEGER, "name" VARCHAR)
// Field "id": INTEGER
// Field "name": VARCHAR
```

#### Output

```
SQL: STRUCT("id" INTEGER, "name" VARCHAR)
Field "id": INTEGER
Field "name": VARCHAR
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewTypeInfo"></a>
### func [NewTypeInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L464>)

```go
func NewTypeInfo(t Type) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewTypeInfo returns type information for DuckDB's primitive types. It returns the TypeInfo, if the Type parameter is a valid primitive type. Else, it returns nil, and an error. Valid types are: TYPE\_\[BOOLEAN, TINYINT, SMALLINT, INTEGER, BIGINT, UTINYINT, USMALLINT, UINTEGER, UBIGINT, FLOAT, DOUBLE, TIMESTAMP, DATE, TIME, INTERVAL, HUGEINT, UHUGEINT, VARCHAR, BLOB, BIT, TIMESTAMP\_S, TIMESTAMP\_MS, TIMESTAMP\_NS, UUID, TIMESTAMP\_TZ, TIME\_TZ\].

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewTypeInfo demonstrates creating TypeInfo for primitive types.

```go
// Create TypeInfo for an integer column
intInfo, err := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_INTEGER,
)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("Type:", intInfo.InternalType())
fmt.Println("SQL:", intInfo.SQLType())

// Create TypeInfo for a varchar column
varcharInfo, err := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_VARCHAR,
)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println(
	"Type:",
	varcharInfo.InternalType(),
)
fmt.Println("SQL:", varcharInfo.SQLType())

// Output:
// Type: INTEGER
// SQL: INTEGER
// Type: VARCHAR
// SQL: VARCHAR
```

#### Output

```
Type: INTEGER
SQL: INTEGER
Type: VARCHAR
SQL: VARCHAR
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewUnionInfo"></a>
### func [NewUnionInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L755-L758>)

```go
func NewUnionInfo(memberTypes []TypeInfo, memberNames []string) (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewUnionInfo returns UNION type information. memberTypes contains the type information of the union members. memberNames contains the names of the union members.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleNewUnionInfo demonstrates creating UNION type information.

```go
// Create member types
intInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_INTEGER,
)
strInfo, _ := dukdb.NewTypeInfo(
	dukdb.TYPE_VARCHAR,
)

// Create the union type
unionInfo, err := dukdb.NewUnionInfo(
	[]dukdb.TypeInfo{intInfo, strInfo},
	[]string{"num", "str"},
)
if err != nil {
	panic(err)
}
fmt.Println("SQL:", unionInfo.SQLType())

// Access union members
details := unionInfo.Details().(*dukdb.UnionDetails)
for _, member := range details.Members {
	fmt.Printf(
		"Member %q: %s\n",
		member.Name,
		member.Type.SQLType(),
	)
}

// Output:
// SQL: UNION("num" INTEGER, "str" VARCHAR)
// Member "num": INTEGER
// Member "str": VARCHAR
```

#### Output

```
SQL: UNION("num" INTEGER, "str" VARCHAR)
Member "num": INTEGER
Member "str": VARCHAR
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewVariantInfo"></a>
### func [NewVariantInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L827>)

```go
func NewVariantInfo() (TypeInfo, error)
```

NewVariantInfo returns VARIANT type information. VARIANT is a dynamic type that can hold any value, stored as JSON string internally.

```go

```

TypesFromSchema extracts DuckDB type information from an Arrow schema.

### func [arrowTypeToDuckDB](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/arrow.go#L630-L632>)

arrowTypeToDuckDB converts an Arrow DataType to a DuckDB TypeInfo. This is the reverse of duckdbTypeToArrow.

<a name="UUID"></a>
## type [UUID](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L27>)

UUID represents a 128\-bit UUID value. Implements sql.Scanner and driver.Valuer interfaces.

```go
type UUID [uuidLength]byte
```

<a name="hugeIntToUUID"></a>
### func [hugeIntToUUID](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/vector.go#L1772>)

```go
func hugeIntToUUID(h *hugeInt) UUID
```



<a name="UUID.Scan"></a>
### func \(\*UUID\) [Scan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L32>)

```go
func (u *UUID) Scan(v any) error
```

Scan implements the sql.Scanner interface. Accepts \[\]byte \(16 bytes\) or string \(UUID format\) as input. NULL values should be handled by the caller; passing nil will result in an error.

<a name="UUID.String"></a>
### func \(\*UUID\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L55>)

```go
func (u *UUID) String() string
```

String implements the fmt.Stringer interface. Returns the UUID in hyphenated format \(xxxxxxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxxxxxxxxxx\).

<a name="UUID.Value"></a>
### func \(\*UUID\) [Value](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L73>)

```go
func (u *UUID) Value() (driver.Value, error)
```

Value implements the driver.Valuer interface. Returns the UUID as a hyphenated string.

<a name="UUIDScanner"></a>
## type [UUIDScanner](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scan_types.go#L340-L342>)

UUIDScanner enables scanning UUID columns to Go UUID types.

```go
type UUIDScanner struct {
    Result *[16]byte
}
```

<a name="UUIDScanner.Scan"></a>
### func \(\*UUIDScanner\) [Scan](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/scan_types.go#L345>)

```go
func (s *UUIDScanner) Scan(src any) error
```

Scan implements the sql.Scanner interface for UUIDScanner.

<a name="Uhugeint"></a>
## type [Uhugeint](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L20-L23>)

Uhugeint represents a 128\-bit unsigned integer. Uses two uint64 values for storage and \*big.Int for arithmetic operations.

```go
type Uhugeint struct {
    lower uint64 // Lower 64 bits
    upper uint64 // Upper 64 bits
}
```

<a name="NewUhugeint"></a>
### func [NewUhugeint](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L27>)

```go
func NewUhugeint(val *big.Int) (Uhugeint, error)
```

NewUhugeint creates a Uhugeint from a \*big.Int. Returns an error if the value is negative or exceeds 128 bits.

<a name="NewUhugeintFromParts"></a>
### func [NewUhugeintFromParts](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L70-L72>)

```go
func NewUhugeintFromParts(upper, lower uint64) Uhugeint
```

NewUhugeintFromParts creates a Uhugeint from upper and lower uint64 values.

<a name="NewUhugeintFromUint64"></a>
### func [NewUhugeintFromUint64](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L65>)

```go
func NewUhugeintFromUint64(val uint64) Uhugeint
```

NewUhugeintFromUint64 creates a Uhugeint from a single uint64.

<a name="Uhugeint.Add"></a>
### func \(Uhugeint\) [Add](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L164-L166>)

```go
func (u Uhugeint) Add(other Uhugeint) (Uhugeint, error)
```

Add adds two Uhugeint values. Returns error on overflow.

<a name="Uhugeint.Cmp"></a>
### func \(Uhugeint\) [Cmp](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L218>)

```go
func (u Uhugeint) Cmp(other Uhugeint) int
```

Cmp compares two Uhugeint values. Returns \-1 if u \< other, 0 if u == other, 1 if u \> other.

<a name="Uhugeint.Div"></a>
### func \(Uhugeint\) [Div](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L201-L203>)

```go
func (u Uhugeint) Div(other Uhugeint) (Uhugeint, error)
```

Div divides u by other. Returns error on division by zero.

<a name="Uhugeint.Equal"></a>
### func \(Uhugeint\) [Equal](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L223>)

```go
func (u Uhugeint) Equal(other Uhugeint) bool
```

Equal returns true if u equals other.

<a name="Uhugeint.IsZero"></a>
### func \(Uhugeint\) [IsZero](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L89>)

```go
func (u Uhugeint) IsZero() bool
```

IsZero returns true if the value is zero.

<a name="Uhugeint.Lower"></a>
### func \(Uhugeint\) [Lower](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L159>)

```go
func (u Uhugeint) Lower() uint64
```

Lower returns the lower 64 bits.

<a name="Uhugeint.Mul"></a>
### func \(Uhugeint\) [Mul](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_extended.go#L190-L192>)

```go
func (u Uhugeint) Mul(other Uhugeint) (Uhugeint, error)
```

Mul multiplies two Uhugeint values. Returns error on overflow.

<a name="Union"></a>
## type [Union](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/types.go#L184-L187>)

Union represents a DuckDB UNION type value. A union contains a single value along with a tag indicating which union member it represents.

```go
type Union struct {
    Value driver.Value `json:"value"`
    Tag   string       `json:"tag"`
}
```


parseUnion parses a DuckDB UNION type. Format: \{"tag": "member\_name", "value": ...\}

```go
type VariantDetails struct{}
```

<a name="VirtualColumnDef"></a>
## type [VirtualColumnDef](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/virtual_table.go#L19-L31>)

VirtualColumnDef represents a column definition for a virtual table.

```go
type VirtualColumnDef struct {
    // Name is the column name.
    Name string

    // Type is the column data type.
    Type Type

    // TypeInfo provides extended type information for complex types.
    TypeInfo TypeInfo

    // Nullable indicates whether the column allows NULL values.
    Nullable bool
}
```

<a name="arrowSchemaToColumns"></a>
### func [arrowSchemaToColumns](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/arrow.go#L1014-L1016>)

```go
func arrowSchemaToColumns(schema *arrow.Schema) ([]VirtualColumnDef, error)
```

arrowSchemaToColumns converts an Arrow schema to a slice of VirtualColumnDef.

<a name="VirtualTable"></a>
## type [VirtualTable](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/virtual_table.go#L6-L16>)

VirtualTable represents a virtual table that can be queried. Virtual tables provide data from external sources \(like Arrow RecordReaders\) without storing the data in the database's storage layer.

```go
type VirtualTable interface {
    // Name returns the table name.
    Name() string

    // Schema returns the table columns as a slice of VirtualColumnDef.
    Schema() []VirtualColumnDef

    // Scan returns an iterator over the table rows.
    // The iterator must be closed after use.
    Scan() (RowIterator, error)
}
```

<a name="VirtualTableRegistry"></a>
## type [VirtualTableRegistry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/virtual_table.go#L51-L65>)

VirtualTableRegistry provides methods for registering and managing virtual tables. Backends can implement this interface to support virtual table registration.

```go
type VirtualTableRegistry interface {
    // RegisterVirtualTable registers a virtual table.
    // Returns an error if a table with the same name already exists.
    RegisterVirtualTable(vt VirtualTable) error

    // UnregisterVirtualTable removes a virtual table.
    // Returns an error if the table does not exist.
    UnregisterVirtualTable(name string) error

    // GetVirtualTable returns a virtual table by name.
    // Returns nil and false if not found.
    GetVirtualTable(
        name string,
    ) (VirtualTable, bool)
}
```

arrowRecordReader implements array.RecordReader for streaming Arrow results.

```go
type arrowRecordReader struct {

    mu          sync.Mutex

    chunkSize   int
    colTypes    []TypeInfo
    colBuilders []array.Builder
}
```

```go

```

Err returns any error that occurred during iteration.


Next advances to the next record batch. Returns true if a record is available, false otherwise.


Record returns the current record batch \(deprecated, use RecordBatch\).


RecordBatch returns the current record batch. Returns nil if Next\(\) has not been called or returned false.


Release decreases the reference count and releases resources when it reaches zero.


Retain increases the reference count.


buildNextBatch builds the next record batch from rows.

```go
func (r *arrowRecordReader) close()
```

close releases all resources.

<a name="parallelTableSource"></a>
## type [parallelTableSource](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L62-L70>)

parallelTableSource is the interface for parallel table sources.

```go
type parallelTableSource interface {
    tableSource
    // Init initializes the table source.
    // Additionally, it returns information for the parallelism-aware table source.
    Init() ParallelTableSourceInfo
    // NewLocalState returns a thread-local execution state.
    // It must return a pointer or a reference type for correct state updates.
    NewLocalState() any
}
```

<a name="placeholder"></a>
## type [placeholder](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/params.go#L17-L28>)

placeholder represents a placeholder in a SQL query.

```go
type placeholder struct {
    // start is the byte offset where the placeholder begins in the query.
    start int
    // end is the byte offset where the placeholder ends in the query.
    end int
    // name is the placeholder name (without $ or @).
    // For positional: "1", "2", etc.
    // For named: the actual name.
    name string
    // isPositional indicates whether this is a positional ($1) or named (@name) placeholder.
    isPositional bool
}
```

<a name="extractNamedPlaceholders"></a>
### func [extractNamedPlaceholders](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/params.go#L84-L86>)

```go
func extractNamedPlaceholders(query string) []placeholder
```

extractNamedPlaceholders extracts named placeholders \(@name\) from a query. It skips placeholders that appear inside string literals \(single\-quoted\).

<a name="extractOrderedParams"></a>
### func [extractOrderedParams](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/prepared.go#L89-L91>)

```go
func extractOrderedParams(query string) []placeholder
```

extractOrderedParams extracts unique parameters from a query and returns them in canonical order. For positional parameters, they are sorted by index \($1, $2, $3\). For named parameters, they are returned in first\-occurrence order.

<a name="extractPositionalPlaceholders"></a>
### func [extractPositionalPlaceholders](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/params.go#L42-L44>)

```go
func extractPositionalPlaceholders(query string) []placeholder
```

extractPositionalPlaceholders extracts positional placeholders \($1, $2, etc.\) from a query. It skips placeholders that appear inside string literals \(single\-quoted\).

## type [tableFunction](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/table_udf.go#L128-L135>)

tableFunction is the generic table function type.

```go
type tableFunction[T tableSource] struct {
    // Config returns the table function configuration, including the function arguments.
    Config TableFunctionConfig
    // BindArguments binds the arguments and returns a TableSource.
    BindArguments func(named map[string]any, args ...any) (T, error)
    // BindArgumentsContext binds the arguments with context and returns a TableSource.
    BindArgumentsContext func(ctx context.Context, named map[string]any, args ...any) (T, error)
}
```

## type [typeInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/type_info.go#L162-L182>)

typeInfo is the internal implementation of TypeInfo.

```go
type typeInfo struct {
    typ Type

    // structEntries holds field metadata for STRUCT types.
    structEntries []StructEntry

    // decimalWidth is the precision for DECIMAL types.
    decimalWidth uint8

    // decimalScale is the scale for DECIMAL types.
    decimalScale uint8

    // arrayLength is the fixed size for ARRAY types.
    arrayLength uint64

    // Member or child types for LIST, MAP, ARRAY, and UNION.
    types []TypeInfo

    // Enum names or UNION member names.
    names []string
}
```

<a name="vector"></a>
## type [vector](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/vector.go#L55-L79>)

vector is the internal columnar storage of a DuckDB column. It stores data in a type\-specific slice with a validity bitmap for NULL handling.

```go
type vector struct {
    // Type metadata.
    vectorTypeInfo

    // dataSlice holds the actual typed data (e.g., []int64, []float64, []string).
    dataSlice any

    // maskBits is the validity bitmap. Bit = 1 means valid, bit = 0 means NULL.
    maskBits []uint64

    // getFn is a callback to get a value from this vector.
    getFn fnGetVectorValue

    // setFn is a callback to set a value in this vector.
    setFn fnSetVectorValue

    // childVectors holds child vectors for nested types (LIST, STRUCT, MAP, ARRAY, UNION).
    childVectors []vector

    // listOffsets stores offsets for LIST type entries.
    listOffsets []uint64

    // capacity is the maximum number of values this vector can hold.
    capacity int
}
```

<a name="newVector"></a>
### func [newVector](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/vector.go#L82>)

```go
func newVector(capacity int) *vector
```

newVector creates a new vector with the specified capacity.

<a name="vector.Close"></a>
### func \(\*vector\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/vector.go#L2145>)

```go
func (vec *vector) Close()
```

Close nils out all slices to allow GC and sets capacity to 0. This prepares the vector for return to a pool.

<a name="vector.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*vector\) [Reset](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/vector.go#L2046>)

```go
func (vec *vector) Reset()
```

Reset clears the vector's data to zero values and marks all entries as valid. It does not reallocate slices, making it suitable for reuse from a pool.

### func \(\*vector\) [clearMask](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/vector.go#L125>)

```go
func (vec *vector) clearMask()
```

clearMask resets all values to NULL. Currently unused but kept for future use in batch operations.


fillMask sets all values to valid \(not NULL\).

<a name="vectorTypeInfo"></a>
## type [vectorTypeInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/vector.go#L27-L51>)

vectorTypeInfo holds type\-specific metadata for a vector.

```go
type vectorTypeInfo struct {
    // Type is the DuckDB type of this vector.
    Type Type

    // structEntries holds field metadata for STRUCT types.
    structEntries []StructEntry

    // decimalWidth is the precision for DECIMAL types.
    decimalWidth uint8

    // decimalScale is the scale for DECIMAL types.
    decimalScale uint8

    // arrayLength is the fixed size for ARRAY types.
    arrayLength int

    // internalType is the internal storage type for ENUM and DECIMAL.
    internalType Type

    // namesDict maps enum/union names to their indices.
    namesDict map[string]uint32

    // tagDict maps union tag indices to names.
    tagDict map[uint32]string
}
```
